races.race_name: KBIS British Equestrian Insurance EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Note To Self
runners.spotlight: Drifted out to 100-30 for last month's debut at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) and was beaten 7l in fifth of six; however, he's with a leading stable and may be able to leave that opening performance miles behind.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on debut but with top yard and could leave that performance miles behind

runners.name: Amir Lehbab
runners.spotlight: 150,000euros foal, 200,000gns yearling; seventh foal; No Nay Never brother to two winners notably Rudi's Apple (6f 2yo; RPR 97), half-brother to three winners including The Vegas Raider (1m2f-2m,; 82); dam Group-placed 7f/1m winner; likely to improve for this first run but respected nevertheless.
runners.comment: 200,000gns yearling; likely to improve for this first run but respected nevertheless

runners.name: Herbert Hall
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros yearling, resold £36,000 yearling; by Starman; first foal; dam unplaced 7f-1m 2yo (RPR 62), sister to winners Agitare (useful 7f-2m Flat and 2m3f jumps) and Make Me Sway (1m), closely related to 6f-7.5f winner Holistic Approach; others debutants make greater appeal on paper.
runners.comment: 8,000euros yearling, resold £36,000 yearling; others debutants make more appeal on paper

runners.name: Keep Grating
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros yearling; eighth foal; Space Traveller half-brother to winners Dazzling Des (5f-6f including 2yo; RPR 85), Merry Secret (6f/7f; 74), Private Affair (5f AW 2yo; 73) and Blue Moon Baby (5f 2yo; 56); dam 5f 2yo winner (91); one of four for the stable; others appeal more.
runners.comment: One of four for the stable; half-brother to four winners but others appeal more

runners.name: Cavalier
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros foal, 95,000euros yearling; third foal; Minzaal half-brother to 7f AW winner They All Know Me (RPR 81); dam 6f winner (including 2yo; 87), half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Dave Dexter; one of four declared runners for Richard Hannon and the betting can be informative on debut.
runners.comment: One of four declared runners for Richard Hannon and the market can be informative on debut

runners.name: Night Star
runners.spotlight: 46,000euros foal, £60,000 yearling; fourth foal; Space Traveller half-brother to 6f winner Smoke Them Out (including 2yo; RPR 86); dam 6f winner (78), half-sister to two winners; one of four for the stable; some of the other newcomers possess more paper appeal.
runners.comment: £60,000 yearling; one of four for yard; some of the other newcomers have more paper appeal

runners.name: Rafe's Da Man
runners.spotlight: Backed into 9-2 for last month's debut at Lingfield (7f, good); interesting to see if his supporters return but he was beaten a long way and needs to be an entirely different proposition this afternoon.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on debut; needs to be an entirely different proposition this afternoon

runners.name: Silver Dominion
runners.spotlight: 200,000euros yearling; third foal; dam French 10.5f winner, half-sister to 7f Group 1 winner Aclaim and smart 1m3f-1m5f winner Aimeric; purchase price catches the eye and this Gleneagles newcomer could run well.
runners.comment: 200,000euros yearling; purchase price catches the eye and this newcomer could run well

runners.name: Sole Ambition
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns foal, 210,000gns yearling; by No Nay Never; second foal; dam Group-placed 6f winner (RPR 98), half-sister to useful 6f 2yo/7f winner Elinor Dashwood; likely to improve for this debut run but not discounted.
runners.comment: 210,000gns yearling; likely to improve for this debut run but not discounted

runners.name: Cloud Summit
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling; first foal; dam runner-up 1m in US, half-sister to Cheveley Park and 1,000 Guineas winner Special Duty; trainer's 2yos are running well and this No Nay Never newcomer is one to watch in the betting.
runners.comment: 100,000gns yearling; trainer's 2yos running well; one to watch in the betting on debut

runners.name: Arabian Blue
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling; second foal; Blue Point half-brother to 1m/1m1f winner I Am Me (RPR 82); dam once-raced half-sister to winners including Emaraaty Ana (6f Group 1), out of 1m4f Listed winner; it's possible he's one for further down the line but is worth a second look given his purchase price.
runners.comment: 150,000gns yearling; possible he's one for further down the line but worth a second look

runners.name: Jimtrott
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns yearling; first foal; dam French Listed-placed 9.5f AW winner (RPR 90), out of maiden half-sister to 1m6f Group 3 winner California; trainer can ready one first time; this No Nay Never newcomer is not ruled out.
runners.comment: 110,000gns yearling; trainer can ready one first time and he's not ruled out

runners.name: Percy Shaw
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Bated Breath brother to Listed-placed 5f winner Lady Tilbury (including 2yo; RPR 100), half-brother to 6f winner Rinky Tinky Tinky (64); dam 5f winner (81), half-sister to 7f Listed winner; no surprise if he gives a good account on debut.
runners.comment: Brother to useful 5f winner Lady Tilbury; no surprise if he gives a good account on debut

races.race_name: Eddie Keogh Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Luminare
runners.spotlight: Won 5f novice at Bath for previous trainer and a 7f handicap on Newcastle AW for this one last December; beaten in three handicaps since and although she might have needed the latest one, when back on turf at Lingfield (7f, good) after a break, she's likely to need more back at 1m.
runners.comment: Won 5f novice and 7f AW handicap; beaten last three starts; stamina to prove back at 1m

runners.name: Suggy
runners.spotlight: Has found his feet in Kempton AW handicaps at 1m on last two starts, just worn down (by one who would have been an unlucky loser) last November, then found a bright turn of foot to win on comeback; up just 4lb but he has a different question to answer this time, returned to turf with rain about.
runners.comment: Two good runs in 1m AW handicaps, impressive latest; this different surface raises doubts

runners.name: Alpine Culture
runners.spotlight: Unraced on slower than good; showed promise in 7f and 1m maidens at Newmarket and Yarmouth in April but out of his depth on handicap debut over 1m2f at the former track and his race was run over 2f out; should have more to offer back at 1m.
runners.comment: Promise in 1m maiden in April; tough handicap debut over 1m2f; should do better back at 1m

runners.name: Slack Bob
runners.spotlight: Kept to 6f and 7f in first five starts; possibly just needed the outing when keeping on at the same pace over 1m at Yarmouth in April and although a subsequent quite impressive win came over 7f on Lingfield AW he shouldn't be written off back over this trip on turf.
runners.comment: May have needed the run on previous 1m attempt; ready 7f winner on AW since
runners.owner: The Old Etonian Racing Syndicate II

runners.name: Box Clever
runners.spotlight: Won an ordinary 7f maiden at Salisbury (good to firm) last August on third outing; had surface queries (heavy then AW) on final 2yo starts but she resumed with a good run at Nottingham (about 1m, good), where she kept persevering when chasing home a next-time winner; can progress further.
runners.comment: Won 7f maiden as 2yo; improved form when 2nd over 1m on comeback behind next-time winner

runners.name: Storm Point
runners.spotlight: All five runs at 7f and 6f on AW, finding extra to hold rivals at bay on handicap debut at Kempton in April, then his path was blocked at Lingfield next time; the step up to 1m may be less of a problem than today's conditions on turf debut.
runners.comment: Won handicap debut on AW (7f) but has a trip and surface query on turf debut

runners.name: Lahan Kingman
runners.spotlight: Good start on 7f debut here as 2yo, keeping on steadily into third behind one who has proved Group-class since; no clear progress in two AW runs (7f and 6f) this year and while the switch to handicap company, back on turf and upped to 1m, makes her of interest, first-time blinkers give pause.
runners.comment: Promising 7f turf debut here; no progress on AW as 3yo; now goes handicapping in headgear

runners.name: Mayflower Billy
runners.spotlight: Best qualifying run over 1m and he found only one too good at Doncaster when back at that trip (good to soft) on handicap debut in May; up 4lb against unexposed rivals so improvement likely to be needed but conditions may be less of a problem for him than some.
runners.comment: Best form at 1m, latest when 2nd on handicap debut; up 4lb; likely more needed

runners.name: The Stone Power
runners.spotlight: Shortish prices despite some difficult assignments in all three AW runs, at 7f and 1m; failed to find much on sole run at this trip but gelded since last seen in January and is worth considering on turf and handicap debut for top yard; market can guide.
runners.comment: Shortish prices in defeat in three AW starts; open to improvement on turf/handicap debut

runners.name: Sydney Rock
runners.spotlight: All three runs at 7f as 2yo, running to a fair level (from the front) on final two starts; pedigree suggests she'll improve for the extra furlong now handicapping but she may also come on for this belated return.
runners.comment: All three runs at 7f; pedigree suggests she'll do better now up in trip; handicap debut

runners.name: You Sexy Thing
runners.spotlight: Showed promise against talented rivals before winning a 7f Sandown handicap (soft) last August, relishing that stiff finish; creditable show in a higher grade at Doncaster after before well held in a valuable sales race last October; gelded since and he should stay 1m; of interest.
runners.comment: Stayed on well for 7f Sandown win on soft; should stay 1m; off since October

races.race_name: Ardonagh Group Handicap

runners.name: I'm Workin On It
runners.spotlight: Made it 4-9 in AW handicaps when winning at Kempton (7f) last month and went close there next time (7f); key player if transferring his form back to grass; well beaten here on sole turf start but that was his debut back in September 2024.
runners.comment: Prolific in AW handicaps; key player if transferring his form back to grass

runners.name: Storm Free
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in the Victoria Cup at Ascot (7f, good) last time but prior to that he made an encouraging reappearance at Newmarket (7f, good) and he won in a big field at Leicester (7f, good) last October; unraced on slow ground; this lightly raced 4yo retains potential and has first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Well beaten in the Victoria Cup but remains lightly raced and retains potential

runners.name: Consecrated
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) last April on debut; hasn't really progressed and has finished merely seventh in 1m6f/1m2f Listed races on her last two starts (tailed off last time); however, it's possible this half-sister to connections' crack 6f/7f performer Sacred will appreciate the drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Hasn't progressed but lightly raced and perhaps she'll appreciate the drop back in trip

runners.name: Forty Years On
runners.spotlight: Close third on debut last March then won her next three, completing the hat-trick on handicap debut at Ascot (7f, soft) in September; head second at Southwell (7f, AW) 16 days later and the winner landed the Hambleton at York last month; makes reappearance and could prove to be well treated.
runners.comment: Progressive in last season's first campaign; makes reappearance and could be well treated

runners.name: Ata Rangi
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season (1m/7f, AW/good) and he added another on last month's reappearance at Haydock (7f, good); his best form has come on good ground but he's shown promise on a slow surface and this progressive 5yo could still have more to offer; respected.
runners.comment: Won on reappearance at Haydock and this progressive 5yo could still have more to offer

runners.name: Best Rate
runners.spotlight: 0-14 since winning in December 2024 on third start but he's been a close fourth over 7f on AW on two of his last three starts, and he ran well on good to soft last August when he last tackled turf (unraced on softer); solid each-way contender.
runners.comment: He's been difficult to win with but often runs well and he's a solid each-way contender

runners.name: Tanmawwy
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way at 50-1 at Salisbury last month on first run since 2024 and this 8yo has to prove how much ability he retains; however, he's on a dangerous mark on his 2024 exploits and might not be a forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way at 50-1 recently after long absence, but dangerous mark; not written off

runners.name: Brian
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on final two runs last year (1m, AW) and same story on his three outings this spring (6f, AW/good/good to firm); arrives with something to prove but he's 5lb lower than when runner-up over C&D (soft) last October and the return to slow ground could facilitate a major revival.
runners.comment: Something to prove about current form but return to slow ground could facilitate a revival

runners.name: Obsidian Dream
runners.spotlight: On a competitive mark on his best form and he's effective on soft ground; this lightly raced 4yo might have needed the run at Bath 16 days ago following a break but he's inconsistent and others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Might have needed the run at Bath but inconsistent and others are more compelling

runners.name: Warm Spell
runners.spotlight: Sole win (from 12 races) came in C&D novice in July 2023; reduced mark appeals on some good efforts last August/September but he's been well below that level on both outings this spring; unproven on slow ground.
runners.comment: On a reduced mark but well below his best on both outings this spring

runners.name: Morte Point
runners.spotlight: Three wins last August/October, the first over C&D (good); also effective on good to soft (unraced on softer); however, he's struggled on his three runs this spring; hopes pinned on wind op prompting dramatic turnaround.
runners.comment: In fine form last August-October but he's struggled so far this season; has had wind op

runners.name: Chalk Mountain
runners.spotlight: Went very close in this last year (good) and, having ended 2025 with a win at Kempton (7f, AW), he reappeared with another there (7f) last month; he can be bang there if handling conditions (unproven on slow ground).
runners.comment: Went very close in this last year and he arrives on a hat-trick; could again be bang there

runners.name: I Still Have Faith
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence but twice went close on AW earlier this year and he was denied a clear run when 2l fourth of 11 at Newmarket (7f, good) last month; seems versatile ground-wise; might not be far away.
runners.comment: On long losing sequence but denied clear run when good fourth of 11 at Newmarket last time
runners.owner: J Morley D Reynolds C Watkins & Partner

runners.name: Spangled Mac
runners.spotlight: 7yo who was well beaten in Bahrain and at Chester on his last two starts; however, he was drawn wide at Chester and showed some good form in Bahrain in January; he's run really well on both previous attempts at this C&D and is not ruled out each-way off a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Return to form needed but had an excuse at Chester and he seems to like it here

runners.name: City Of Kings
runners.spotlight: Three runs for Charlie Appleby up to April 2023, winning at Ascot on the middle outing; gelded since and sold for 140,000 UAE Dirham in March 2024; on a handy mark if retaining his past promise and he's one to watch in the betting on stable debut, but it's a very hefty absence to defy.
runners.comment: Ex-Godolphin; watch betting on stable debut but has a very hefty absence to defy

runners.name: Nostrum
runners.spotlight: Has considerable back-class (including on soft ground) and he's shown promise on both starts for this yard, most recently runner-up at Sandown (1m, good); high on the list on the back of that performance.
runners.comment: Considerable back-class and promise on both starts for this yard; high on the list

races.race_name: Tonight's Super Boost Live At BetVictor Handicap

runners.name: Hockney
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who landed a gamble on his handicap debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) last September and made short work of following up at Thirsk one week later; slow start to his 2026 campaign but things clicked back on AW at Kempton three weeks ago, gamely repelling the reopposing Moving Force; could progress further after just eight runs.
runners.comment: Front-runner who is 3-5 in handicaps; could have more to offer

runners.name: Pressure's On
runners.spotlight: Usually seen over further but he goes well on slow ground and is only 1lb higher than for his big-field win at York last summer (7f, good to soft); big run wouldn't be a bolt from the blue.
runners.comment: Having a rare sprint run but he'll love the ground and lurks on a handy mark

runners.name: Wild Clary
runners.spotlight: A Southwell maiden win last summer remains his only success after 15 attempts but he has run good races in defeat in all three starts this year; conditions should suit well and he is more appealing than most.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate but arrives in fine form and conditions should suit; solid claims

runners.name: Kiniro
runners.spotlight: Won twice on Lingfield's AW track when trained by Harry Charlton; promising stable debut at Chester last month (met trouble from a poor draw) and although he couldn't land a blow at York last time, he didn't run poorly by any stretch; Oisin Murphy back in the saddle; slower than good would be a query.
runners.comment: 2 AW wins for H Charlton; promising yard debut but not so good at York latest; ground a ?

runners.name: Golden Long
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained in Hong Kong; gambled on ahead of his British debut at Southwell in February (5f) and shaped with plenty of promise; only third at Yarmouth in April but he made no mistake at Windsor last month (6f, good to firm; runner-up has won since); the combination of a  6lb rise and slower ground may find him out.
runners.comment: 6f win at Windsor last month but up 6lb and likely to face much slower ground

runners.name: Righthere Rightnow
runners.spotlight: 0-12 since his wide-margin debut win as a 2yo but he ended 2025 with some good runs and went close in Dubai in December (6f, good to firm); his last two runs at Meydan weren't his best but a break may have freshened him up and he is on a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: 0-12 since debut win but his best handicap efforts bring him right into the reckoning

runners.name: Justcallmepete
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; five wins since joining Tony Carroll, including turf handicaps in two of his last three starts; should give his running once again but may prove vulnerable for win purposes.
runners.comment: C&D winner who arrives in top form; creeping back up the weights; may prove vulnerable

runners.name: Never Just A Dream
runners.spotlight: Smart performer on AW for Ismail Mohammed; one good run and two modest efforts for Alice Haynes last year; potentially thrown in now starting out for the excellent Terry Kent but he does have to prove himself on turf (stiff tasks on last two attempts).
runners.comment: Starts out for new yard off a dangerous mark but has to prove his effectiveness on turf

runners.name: Moving Force
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 5f juvenile winner for Richard Fahey; low-key stable debut last month but fared much better when second of ten behind the reopposing Hockney at Kempton (6f; 50-1) three weeks ago; best turf form achieved on faster ground.
runners.comment: Back to form when second to Hockney on AW last month; best turf form on faster ground

races.race_name: HMC Horse Boxes EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tarrant
runners.spotlight: Third in a hot newcomers' race here (7f, good) in April; another good performance when third in slowly run maiden at Ascot (1m2f, good) last month; she's a leading form player today.
runners.comment: Third in good races here and at Ascot on her two starts and she's a leading form player

runners.name: Manaar
runners.spotlight: 175,000gns foal, 370,000gns yearling; fifth foal; St Mark's Basilica half-sister to three winners, notably the stable's Dubai Honour (prolific 1m-1m4f including Group 1); intriguing newcomer who could have a big part to play.
runners.comment: Half-sister to the stable's Group 1 winner Dubai Honour; could have a big part to play

runners.name: Shady Dame
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on debut at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last September and not disgraced when sixth of ten in Group 3 Oh So Sharp Stakes there (7f, good) in October; her dam was a 1m6f Group 3 winner and the step up in trip could be a good move on her reappearance; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Promise over 7f on both 2yo starts and the step up in trip could be a good move on return

runners.name: Miss Scott
runners.spotlight: 650,000gns yearling who has an attractive pedigree and readily made a winning debut at Leicester (7f, good to soft) last September; her breeding suggests this longer trip will suit and she's a strong contender under a penalty; wears a first-time hood.
runners.comment: Very likeable debut win at Leicester last September; strong contender under a penalty

runners.name: Pierpoint
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and 40-1 this spring on her first two starts (7f/1m2f, good); she's shown ability but improvement is needed to come out on top today.
runners.comment: She's shown ability on both starts but improvement is needed to come out on top today

runners.name: Trelissick
runners.spotlight: Encouraging fourth of 14 on debut at Kempton (7f, AW) in April; didn't build on that when seventh of ten at Windsor (1m, good) last month but she stumbled leaving the stalls and could get closer to the target today.
runners.comment: Encouraging fourth on debut at Kempton and she stumbled early when seventh at Windsor

runners.name: Hatta Romance
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns 2yo; by Frankel; second foal; dam 7f/1m 2yo winner (including Group 2), out of Listed-placed 1m 2yo winner; makes debut with yard among the winners but a watching brief is probably wisest.
runners.comment: Well bred; bought for 22,000gns last October; a watching brief is probably wisest on debut

runners.name: Aunt Roberta
runners.spotlight: 85,000euros yearling; tenth foal; Sea The Stars sister to three winners including Fifty Stars (Australian 6f-1m2f including Group 1), half-sister to winners Revolver (1m4f-2m1f; RPR 107) and Whiskey Sour (7f-2m1f Flat; 105); market check advised on debut but a useful standard is needed.
runners.comment: Debut; sister to Australian Group 1 winner; market check advised but good standard needed

runners.name: Pseudonym
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; Australia half-sister to 7f/1m 2yo winner Ghostwriter (including Group 2); dam Listed-placed 1m4f AW winner; makes debut in a warm contest but it will be interesting to see how she goes in the betting.
runners.comment: Half-sister to class act Ghostwriter; interesting to see how she goes in betting on debut

runners.name: Tropical Sands
runners.spotlight: 28-1 and beaten just over 7l when fourth of 12 on last month's debut at Lingfield (1m, AW); however, she made some late headway and could be sharper for that experience; not ruled out over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: 28-1, no threat on her debut but made late headway and could be sharper for the experience
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Mountain Of Light
runners.spotlight: She cost 425,000gns in 2024 and her inexperience was evident when well beaten at 11-1 on debut at Haydock (1m, good to firm) in April; however, she's probably best watched until showing some promise.
runners.comment: 425,000gns yearling; inexperience evident on debut; probably best watched for now

races.race_name: White Horse International Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Mythical Bay
runners.spotlight: Won Wolverhampton novice (9.4f, AW) in January on fourth start and returned from break with close second on handicap debut at Chester (1m4f, good) last month; only third of four at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) but that was still a good run and he could be in the shake-up today.
runners.comment: Positive performances last month in first two handicaps and he could be in the shake-up

runners.name: Kindly Queen
runners.spotlight: Close fourth on handicap debut at Kempton (1m4f, AW) in March and runner-up in first-time blinkers (retained) at Windsor since (11.4f, good to firm; first turf start); in good hands to continue to progress; key player.
runners.comment: She's run really well in defeat in her first two handicaps and she's a key player

runners.name: Hard To Believe
runners.spotlight: Won at Bath (1m, good to soft) last October on second start and followed up in Wolverhampton novice (9.4f, AW) in March; unplaced favourite on handicap debut at York (1m4f, good) four weeks ago but he retains potential and it will be no surprise if he poses far more of a threat today.
runners.comment: Unplaced favourite on handicap debut at York but retains potential and could be a threat
runners.owner: Jayar Investments & Mrs L E Ramsden

runners.name: Impossible Mission
runners.spotlight: Won on nursery debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) last November; flopped on his first two runs this year but back on song with 5l third of 11 at Haydock (1m2f, good to soft) three weeks ago, when shaping as though this step up in trip could be a good move; respected.
runners.comment: Kept on for third at Haydock last month and this longer trip could prove to be a positive

runners.name: Barrister
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth at Kempton (7f, AW) on sole 2yo start and, while he's been well beaten in novices here (1m2f, good) and at Kempton (1m3f, AW) on both runs this year, he's been behind classy winners; has far more realistic claims on this handicap debut; interesting, especially if backed.
runners.comment: Has far more realistic claims on this handicap debut; interesting, especially if backed

runners.name: Glenna
runners.spotlight: Produced best run yet when fifth of 14, beaten just under 3l, over 1m2f here (good) four weeks ago having made the running; has also shown promise on soft ground; however, she needs something extra today to get off the mark.
runners.comment: Respectable 5th of 14 here four weeks ago but needs something extra today to get off mark

runners.name: Honouramongthieves
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and he needs to produce more than he's managed in Ireland this spring in his first two handicaps (1m4f/1m3f, good to yielding/good); however, his trainer has an excellent 13-41 (32%) record with his British raiders in the last five years; firmly in calculations in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: 0-6 and needs to find extra but his Irish yard has excellent record with British raiders

runners.name: Cosmo Brown
runners.spotlight: Down the field at big odds this spring on first two runs (1m, good) but showed improved form in a first-time hood (retained) when third at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) last time; the manner in which he kept on provides optimism for 1m4f and he's one to consider on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Kept on for third in 1m2f novice at Leicester; one to consider up to 1m4f on h'cap debut

races.race_name: QuinnBet Second To The Favourite "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Part Of Racing Excellence)

runners.name: Heretic
runners.spotlight: First 27 starts came on the AW (three wins) and was making his turf debut when third of 13 at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) last month (fourth has won twice since); 5lb below last winning mark and much respected for stable that won this in 2024.
runners.comment: Third on belated turf debut at Doncaster last time; 5lb below last winning mark; respected

runners.name: Sedgemoor
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner on the AW this year, but does have winning form on turf; needs to put a poor effort at Doncaster last month (7f, good to soft) behind him, though, and probably wouldn't want rain.
runners.comment: Dual winner on the AW this year; needs to put a poor effort behind him; wouldn't want rain

runners.name: Bear To Dream
runners.spotlight: A bit in and out since winning narrowly from 2lb lower at Chepstow (7f, good) last July; could do with the ground drying out.
runners.comment: A bit in and out since winning at Chepstow last July; could do with the ground drying out

runners.name: Rusheen Boy
runners.spotlight: Four of his five wins have come over 6f/7f on the AW, but the other came in emphatic fashion here (6f, good to firm) a year ago; probably ran even better than it looked when third of ten at Lingfield (7f, good) last month and a major player for last year's winning stable.
runners.comment: 6f course winner and stays this trip well; major player for last year's winning stable

runners.name: Divine Wish
runners.spotlight: All four starts have come over 7f on the AW and she has shown ability, as when fourth of 12 on handicap debut at Kempton last month; still unexposed and respected if showing similar form on turf.
runners.comment: All four starts have come on the AW and has shown ability; respected if effective on turf

runners.name: Cooramook
runners.spotlight: 0-18; has made the frame on several occasions, but 7f on a straight track like this may just stretch her.
runners.comment: 0-18; has made the frame a few times, but 7f on a straight track may just stretch her

races.race_name: Paul Corrigan Memorial Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Nevasca Cinza
runners.spotlight: In the frame in all four starts over 6f, but was sent off odds-on when only third of six here (good) last time; doesn't appear to be progressing, but is still shortlisted on form and cheekpieces are now given a go.
runners.comment: In the frame in all four starts; not progressing but still shortlisted; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Court Alert
runners.spotlight: Looked a winner waiting to happen when second of 20 in a sales race at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last August, but heavy defeats in both outings since need forgiving (gelded before latest start); has undergone wind surgery since last seen.
runners.comment: Twice disappointing since finishing runner-up in a Newmarket sales race; had a wind op

runners.name: Upper Hendersyde
runners.spotlight: 220,000gns yearling; 9-2 when second of 12 on his Salisbury debut (6f, good) last month; not sure how stong the form is with the third horse finishing a well beaten third of four at Leicester on Monday, but he is open to improvement.
runners.comment: Just beaten on his Salisbury debut last month; form not yet boosted, but he may improve

runners.name: Anyasi
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling; sent off 14-1 when beaten 6l into third of six behind the subsequent Greenham winner Alparslan on his Leicester debut (7f, good to firm) last August, showing signs of inexperience; the form has also been franked by those behind as well so he has to be taken seriously on his return.
runners.comment: Third of six on his Leicester debut last August; form has worked out well; taken seriously

runners.name: Debrief
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns foal; well held in two novices (6f/7f) seven months apart; handicaps over further may be more suitable after this.
runners.comment: Well held in two novices seven months apart; handicaps over further may be more suitable

runners.name: Red Legs
runners.spotlight: Missed the break and took a keen hold when eighth of ten on her Windsor debut (6f, good to firm; 40-1) ten days ago; may need more time.
runners.comment: Missed the break and took a keen hold when eighth of ten on her Windsor debut ten days ago

runners.name: Rogue Federation
runners.spotlight: 40,000euros foal; 45,000gns yearling; half-brother to five winners including Summer Daydream (6f including 2yo Listed/AW; RPR 96), Cityzen Serg (8.6f AW; 91), Initial Blue (6f AW including 2yo; 76) and Summer Lady (Italian 7f-1m1f); dam placed 6f-7f (including 2yo/AW; 78); already gelded but worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Half-brother to five winners; already gelded but worth monitoring in the market

runners.name: Yazoo
runners.spotlight: £50,000 yearling; dam 5f/6f AW winner (including 2yo; RPR 103), closely related to 1m Listed winner Zurigha, half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Lily's Angel, out of unraced half-sister to French 10.5f Group 3 winner In Clover; already gelded but his pedigree makes him well worth a market check.
runners.comment: Already gelded but his pedigree makes him well worth a market check

runners.name: Unique Procedure
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and well beaten in two starts over 6f on good ground in April, the latest here (hooded both times); best watched for now.
runners.comment: Well beaten in both starts in April; best watched for now

runners.name: George Wickham
runners.spotlight: Ran green when last of seven on his Bath debut (5.7f, firm; 12-1) last August; major improvement required.
runners.comment: Ran green when last of seven on his Bath debut last August; major improvement required

races.race_name: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap

runners.name: Neyva's Angel
runners.spotlight: Won at Southwell (6f, AW) in April and was only beaten a head here (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago with a yawning gap back to the rest; 4lb rise not the end of the world and good to soft ground wouldn't be a problem, but another furlong may have been preferable.
runners.comment: Beaten a head over 6f here a fortnight ago; another furlong may have been preferable

runners.name: Travel Agent
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come over 6f on the AW including two in April; handles turf, but he has a fair bit to find with Neyva's Angel on their meeting here (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; he too may be better over further.
runners.comment: Has a fair bit to find with Neyva's Angel on their meeting here a fortnight ago

runners.name: Thunder Star
runners.spotlight: All five wins have come over 5f on turf and was only beaten a head at Thirsk (5f, good) six days ago; 1lb well in and won't mind what the weather does; major player.
runners.comment: All five wins have come over 5f on turf; 1lb well in and won't mind what the weather does

runners.name: Al Barez
runners.spotlight: Losing run up to 15, but has been running well in defeat as when beaten less than a length into third of ten at Windsor (5f, good) last month; races off the same mark, but he is another for whom this trip looks sharp enough.
runners.comment: Running well in defeat lately, but is another for whom this trip looks sharp enough

runners.name: Existent
runners.spotlight: Finally ended a 49-race losing run at Southwell (5f, AW) in January; now 2lb lower than for that success and this company is much easier than that he normally keeps, but his record of 1-47 on turf still makes him hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Takes on lesser company, but his record of 1-47 on turf makes him hard to warm to

races.race_name: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap

runners.name: Chasing Gold
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame seven times, but now 0-14 and is virtually reappearing from six months off having unseated his rider exiting the stalls at Nottingham last month; that wasn't the first time he has had issues at the start so is probably best watched; blinkers given a go.
runners.comment: 0-14; unseated his rider at the start last time and probably best watched; blinkers on

runners.name: Walking On Clouds
runners.spotlight: Last 23 starts have come on the AW and he is 4lb well in under a penalty for his emphatic success in refitted blinkers at Lingfield (6f) ten days ago; he has won twice on turf, though, so the change in surface shouldn't be an issue; major player.
runners.comment: 4lb well in under a penalty for his emphatic success on the Lingfield AW ten days ago

runners.name: Coolagh Magic
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is 9-46 on the AW, but 0-22 on turf while his losing run is up to 16 despite a plummeting mark; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-22 on turf while his losing run is up to 16 despite a plummeting mark; others preferred
runners.owner: Lady Emma Oshea And A Carson

runners.name: Top Star
runners.spotlight: Finished 8l behind Walking On Clouds at Lingfield (6f, AW) ten days ago, taking his record to 0-14; only 4lb better off and has finished out of the frame in all five starts on turf; tongue-tie needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: 0-14 and finished 8l behind Walking On Clouds at Lingfield ten days ago; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Prefer The Sister
runners.spotlight: 1-24, the win coming at Kempton  (7f, AW) at the end of 2024; makes her stable debut after 101 days off having changed hands for 2,200gns in April; not sure what to expect on only her second go over a trip this short.
runners.comment: Stable debut; not sure what to expect on her second go over a trip this short

runners.name: Mighty Ruler
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 20 starts came off this mark at Lingfield (7f, AW) in January; turf form not so hot including a fall at Lingfield last month; others more persuasive.
runners.comment: Sole win from 20 starts came on the AW in January; turf form not so hot

runners.name: Court Of Session
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner who was getting off the mark on turf at the 16th attempt when successful at Nottingham (6f, good) eight days ago; however, he was well beaten over this C&D (soft) yesterday.
runners.comment: Off the mark on turf at Nottingham eight days ago but well beaten over this C&D yesterday

runners.name: Tilsworth Turf
runners.spotlight: Both wins came over 6f on soft ground in 2024, but he's mostly struggled at big odds since the latest of them; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Mostly struggled at big odds since the latest of his two wins; best watched for now

runners.name: Mr Phillips
runners.spotlight: 0-7, though he ended last term with much his best performance when third of 16 over C&D (good to soft; 125-1) in October; off eight months since, but would hold each-way claims if picking up where he left off; stable also runs Tilsworth Turf.
runners.comment: Third at huge odds over C&D on final start last term; each-way claims on that form

races.race_name: QuinnBet Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: High Point
runners.spotlight: Back off the same mark as when successful at Haydock (1m6f, good to soft) last September; Flat form since has been patchy but still one for the shortlist, especially if the ground stays on the soft side.
runners.comment: Back off last winning mark; shortlisted, especially if the ground stays on the soft side

runners.name: Fox Avatar
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 1m2f/1m4f on the AW last summer and probably needed his Kempton reappearance 15 days ago having been off for nine months; won on turf as a 3yo and worth considering back in a Class 4.
runners.comment: Won twice on AW last summer and won on turf as a 3yo; worth considering back in Class 4

runners.name: First Officer
runners.spotlight: Goes on any ground; didn't run at all badly when fifth of ten at Salisbury (1m4f, good) 19 days ago having been off for eight months (has run well fresh); now 1lb below his last winning mark and considered.
runners.comment: Goes on any ground; now 1lb below his last winning mark and considered

runners.name: Yokohama
runners.spotlight: Was winning for the first time since his debut when just getting the better of Patrol at Newbury (1m4f, good) last month; 5lb higher and could probably do with the ground drying out (best form on good or faster), but still respected.
runners.comment: Won at Newbury last month; 5lb higher and could probably do with the ground drying out

runners.name: Moonrunner
runners.spotlight: Progressive in his first three starts, beaten a length on turf debut in a Haydock maiden (11.6f, soft) three weeks ago; could well continue to improve now handicapping and one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Progressive in his first three starts; could well continue to improve now handicapping

runners.name: Newtown Duke
runners.spotlight: 1-2 on the AW and 0-9 on turf, though in the frame seven times as when third of 15 in a Chester apprentice handicap (1m4f, good) on his reappearance last month; a couple of those in behind have won since and he makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: In the frame in seven of his nine starts on turf; makes the shortlist

runners.name: Patrol
runners.spotlight: 3-13 on the AW and 0-12 on turf, though he was only beaten half a length by Yokohama at Newbury (1m4f, good) last month (third has won since); 4lb rise for that does make things a bit harder, though.
runners.comment: Only beaten half a length at Newbury last month; 4lb rise for that does make things harder

runners.name: Baltic
runners.spotlight: Five-timer for the Charlton stable between September 2023 and July 2024 included a win over C&D, but he was beaten out of sight on his stable debut/reappearance at Kempton 15 days ago; has plenty to prove now.
runners.comment: C&D winner, but was beaten out of sight on his stable debut at Kempton 15 days ago

races.race_name: Download The QuinnBet App Classified Stakes

runners.name: Dors Delight
runners.spotlight: 1-17 on the AW, the win coming in a similar contest to this at Chelmsford (1m2f) last August; hard to know how she will cope on her belated turf debut, though.
runners.comment: 1-17 on the AW; hard to know how she will cope on her belated turf debut

runners.name: Sporty Socks
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who was twice placed on the AW last year, but well held in all five starts on turf, the latest over C&D last month; drop in grade needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Well held in all five starts on turf; drop in grade needs to make a difference

runners.name: Zafaan
runners.spotlight: 2-28, his latest success coming in a similar contest over C&D (good) just under a year ago; a player if producing his best form.
runners.comment: 2-28, his latest success coming in a similar contest over C&D just under a year ago

runners.name: Wrist Art
runners.spotlight: Both wins came in Ireland in the autumn of 2023; not beaten far in his last two starts in 0-70 handicaps at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) and Southwell (1m3f, AW) and even if those efforts flatter him, they still make impressive reading at this level.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in 0-70 handicaps in his last two starts; impressive reading at this level

runners.name: Wingstar
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who showed a little ability on the AW at the start of the year, but her two efforts for this yard leave plenty to be desired.
runners.comment: Showed a little ability at the start of the year, but moderate in two starts for this yard

runners.name: Sunset In Paris
runners.spotlight: All six wins have come at around this trip on the AW; moderate effort over hurdles last month and withdrawn in that code subsequently having been unruly at the post; best watched.
runners.comment: All six wins have come at around this trip on the AW; best watched

runners.name: Sapphire Dream
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in ten starts over 5f-1m on turf/AW in Ireland; makes his stable debut after 132 days off having been picked up for 1,000gns in February; drops in class, but still needs more.
runners.comment: Unplaced in ten starts in Ireland; makes his stable debut after 132 days off; needs more

runners.name: Rockafeller Skank
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in three starts over 7f last autumn; having been gelded, he reappeared in a 1m classified here (soft) yesterday and won with something to spare, also shaping as though he would be suited by today's extra distance; has to be respected under 6lb penalty if running again.
runners.comment: Reappeared here yesterday and won with something to spare; has to be respected

runners.name: Churchill's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Well held in three starts over 7f on turf/AW late last year; off six months, but this trip is more in line with his pedigree and worth a second look in the first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Well held in three starts late last year, but this trip may suit better; tongue-tie on
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Lvi & Mrs G Jarvis

runners.name: Sweetstevie
runners.spotlight: Big prices and has finished out the back in three starts at Southwell (1m, AW) early this year; switch to turf and first-time visor need to make a dramatic difference.
runners.comment: Out the back in three starts on the AW at Southwell; visor on

runners.name: Trackman
runners.spotlight: 0-24 under rules, but has made the frame three times in eight visits here; could sneak into the frame again.
runners.comment: 0-24 under rules, but has made the frame three times in eight visits here

races.race_name: quinnbet.com Handicap

runners.name: Cospicua
runners.spotlight: 2-4 in AW handicaps with both wins coming over 1m4f at Kempton, but she was well beaten in the other two; not quite sure what to expect on turf debut, but she is the only previous winner in the field.
runners.comment: 2-4 in AW handicaps; not sure what to expect on turf debut, but she knows how to win

runners.name: Darkest Red
runners.spotlight: Gradually getting there, beaten under a length here (1m2f, good) in April and going down by a short head in a 14-runner event at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) last month, finishing nicely clear of the rest; up 4lb, but still looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: 4lb higher than when beaten a short head at Windsor last month, but still the one to beat

runners.name: Premier Cru
runners.spotlight: Has regressed in her four starts and finished miles behind Tenison at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft) last month; needs a turnaround.
runners.comment: Has regressed in four starts and finished miles behind Tenison at Nottingham last month

runners.name: Picaro
runners.spotlight: Best effort in three starts when fourth of eight in a Southwell novice (1m3f, AW) in April; could show more now handicapping with cheekpieces applied.
runners.comment: Unplaced in three starts; could show more now handicapping with cheekpieces applied

runners.name: Queen Of Astolat
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit more when sixth of ten on handicap debut at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) last month; more needed, but she may come on for that first start in 135 days.
runners.comment: Showed a bit more when sixth of ten at Windsor last month; may have come on for the run

runners.name: Salamis Bay
runners.spotlight: Some ability in her first two starts over 6f on the AW and faced an impossible task at Ascot (1m2f, good; 125-1) last month; still open to improvement on handicap debut and may show a bit more.
runners.comment: Unplaced in three starts but still open to improvement on handicap debut

runners.name: Tenison
runners.spotlight: Appeared to improve when fourth of 11 on handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft) last month, but still finished over 10l behind the winner; further improvement needed, but stepping up again in trip may help.
runners.comment: Unplaced in four starts; further improvement needed, but stepping up in trip may help

runners.name: Bennyworth
runners.spotlight: Best effort when second of six on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) in April, but he didn't get home on turf debut there (1m2f, good to firm) ten days ago, which is a worry stepping up again in trip; stable also runs Darkest Red.
runners.comment: Didn't get home on turf debut over 1m2f at Lingfield ten days ago, which is a worry

runners.name: Home Secretary
runners.spotlight: Beat a total of one rival in four starts over 7f/1m on turf/AW last autumn; gelded since and makes his handicap debut in first-time cheekpieces after 227 days off; market should be revealing.
runners.comment: Makes his handicap debut in first-time cheekpieces after 227 days off; market revealing

races.race_name: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C & D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Priscilla's Hope
runners.spotlight: Modest form shown in her three runs thus far, fifth of nine in 6f Redcar maiden 16 days ago; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Modest form shown in her three runs thus far; needs to take a big step forward

runners.name: Moriarty Moon
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner colt who still looked green when seventh of 11 in 6f Chester novice 12 days ago; can take another step forward here.
runners.comment: Still looked green when seventh at Chester 12 days ago; can take another step forward

runners.name: Nascent Star
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to French/Spanish 5.5f-7f winner Fiction; dam, unraced half-sister to winners Azmeel (1m2f Group 3) and Baisse (German 1m Listed), is out of a useful 7f/1m winner; yard's juveniles are going very well so this Starman colt rates an interesting prospect.
runners.comment: Yard's juveniles are going very well so this Starman colt rates an interesting prospect

runners.name: Agnes Hathaway
runners.spotlight: Oscar Performance filly; still looked in need of the experience when seventh of nine in 5.5f Bath maiden 16 days ago; she's the sort to do better in due course.
runners.comment: Still in need of experience when seventh at Bath latest; sort to do better in due course
runners.owner: D Kilburn, C Wright, F Mahony & Partner

runners.name: Barnaby Rudge
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns 2yo; Inns Of Court colt; half-brother to 5f winner Miss Yechance (RPR 92); dam, 5f 2yo winner (RPR 82), is a half-sister to US 8.5f stakes winner Fuente Ovejuna, out of a maiden half-sister to French 1m2f Group 1 winner Jemayel; hooded for his debut but in good hands and needs considering.
runners.comment: Inns Of Court colt; hooded for his debut but in good hands and needs considering

runners.name: Blondie Larry
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros yearling; Showcasing colt; dam, Group-placed 6f winner (RPR 101), is out of an unraced half-sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner Claremont; appeals on paper so he can't be ruled out, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Showcasing debutant who can't be ruled out, especially if the market vibes are positive

runners.name: Dreamlover
runners.spotlight: Sent off 13-8 for his debut but looked in need of the experience when fourth of six in 5f Southwell maiden last month; since gelded and this son of Cotai Glory is clearly thought capable of better.
runners.comment: Sent off 13-8 when debut fourth at Southwell; since gelded and thought capable of better

runners.name: Wolf's Gallop
runners.spotlight: 33-1 but hinted at promise with debut seventh of nine in maiden at Doncaster (5f, good to firm) 26 days ago; this son of Sergei Prokofiev can build on it now.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise with debut seventh at Doncaster; can build on it now

runners.name: One In A Millz
runners.spotlight: 12-1 when last of 12 on his debut in a maiden at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 19 days ago; this son of Bungle Inthejungle needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Last of 12 on debut at Windsor; needs to take a major step forward

runners.name: Shaniko
runners.spotlight: Hooded for her debut when last of 12 in seller at Chester (6f, good) 12 days ago; significantly more is required.
runners.comment: Hooded for her debut when last of 12 in seller at Chester 12 days ago

races.race_name: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Fractional
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on both runs as a 2yo, third in 1m Yarmouth maiden in October; found the step up to Listed level too much on her return when fifth of six at Newbury (1m2f), but this daughter of Frankel remains with plenty of potential; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Step up to Listed level too much when fifth at Newbury; remains with plenty of potential

runners.name: Decade Of Time
runners.spotlight: Study Of Man colt who shaped really well with a debut Newbury third before cosily landing 11-runner novice at Windsor (1m) last month; has to concede weight all round but looks to have more to offer; big player.
runners.comment: Built on debut promise to land Windsor novice; looks to have more to offer so a big player

runners.name: Odogwu
runners.spotlight: 20-1, made a very encouraging start when fifth of 15 in a novice at Newbury (7f, good) last month, fading only late on; this son of Earthlight is open to a fair bit of progress so needs considering.
runners.comment: Very encouraging start with Newbury fifth; open to a fair bit of progress so considered

runners.name: Prince Of Calypso
runners.spotlight: Shaped well under a patient ride with a debut third of ten in 7f Haydock maiden in September; since gelded (cheekpieces also added) and this son of Calyx seems sure to take a step forward on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Shaped well with debut Haydock third in September; since gelded and can take step forward

races.race_name: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap

runners.name: Wondrous Light
runners.spotlight: She has looked an improved performer this year, winning at Beverley on her reappearance (8.5f, good to soft) and adding a C&D fillies' handicap last month (good to soft); up in class and now taking on male opposition but she shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: 2-3 this year, including C&D last month; up in class against male opposition today

runners.name: Return To Unit
runners.spotlight: Improved with each run as a 3yo, getting off the mark in a C&D maiden last October (good ground); couldn't land a blow on his seasonal/handicap debut at Ayr (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago, but still has potential and blinkers could help unlock it.
runners.comment: C&D win last October; midfield on seasonal/handicap debut; now sports blinkers; unexposed

runners.name: On The River
runners.spotlight: Promising fifth in the Spring Mile on his return and then found only a stablemate too strong at Ripon in April (both 1m, good to soft); not so good at Pontefract last time when faced with good ground; the more rain to fall the better for him.
runners.comment: Below par on good ground latest but on a fair mark if the rain arrives

runners.name: One Night Thunder
runners.spotlight: All wins over 7f, but he did run well over C&D in June 2023 and was staying on at the finish at Haydock (7f, good) last month; significant rainfall would aid his claims.
runners.comment: All wins at 7f but worth another crack at this trip; rain would aid his cause

runners.name: Triple Double A
runners.spotlight: Reeled off a fast-ground 1m hat-trick last spring; better than ever when landing a 1m2f handicap at Newcastle on his seasonal return in February; no further progress and he drops back to 1m on a recovery mission; today's drop in grade will suit.
runners.comment: Not kicked on from reappearance win (1m2f, AW) but eased in grade this afternoon

runners.name: Miletus
runners.spotlight: Just one win to his name after 14 starts but he has performed with credit in four runs for his new stable, including second-place finishes at Lingfield (1m, AW) and Doncaster (7f good to soft); hood experiment quickly aborted; should go well again.
runners.comment: Four solid runs for new trainer; hood left off this time; should give another good account

runners.name: Swift Salian
runners.spotlight: Two solid runner-up efforts in 2026 and he won't mind what the weather does; sneaking up the weights but no obvious reason why he wouldn't give it another good go.
runners.comment: Two good runs this year and he won't mind what the weather does; contender

runners.name: Youarenotforgiven
runners.spotlight: Was recording wins eight, nine and ten when rattling off an AW hat-trick this spring (1m-8.6f); the wheels came off when well-beaten over C&D 33 days ago, however, and this mark looks beyond him.
runners.comment: AW hat-trick this spring but well beaten over C&D last month; looks too high in weights

runners.name: San Juanito
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins for the Crisfords but much more to prove on turf; absent since a midfield finish on his stable debut in December; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Two AW wins but far more to prove on turf; absent six months

races.race_name: Watch On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Caraway
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when winning a Class 5 handicap at Thirsk last month (1m4f, good), travelling strongly but taking a while to pick up before seeing her race out well; up 5lb but she's bred to be smart and this additional distance looks a big plus; strong contender.
runners.comment: Strong finish to win a Class 5 (1m4f, good) at Thirsk last month; new trip a plus; solid

runners.name: Gemical
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts in 1m4f handicaps on her last two runs, the latest at Catterick when tried in a visor for the first time; dropped 3lb and now tackles a longer trip; place claims again.
runners.comment: New trip could spark extra and she has been dropped 3lb since finishing third 12 days ago
runners.owner: G C Gale & Stuart Brown & Diane Stewart

runners.name: Riddikulus
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in her six runs at up to 1m2f; some encouragement for this new trip on breeding and could yet have a bigger effort in her.
runners.comment: Not solid judged on six runs at up to 1m2f; encouragement for staying trips on pedigree

runners.name: Sir Griflet
runners.spotlight: Best effort to date when a close fourth at Wolverhampton nine days ago (1m4f), headed in the home straight and losing two more places close home; steps up in trip with cheekpieces added; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Not beaten far into 4th at Wolverhampton nine days ago (1m4f); cheekpieces now added

runners.name: Fairydale
runners.spotlight: Little impact since handicapping and when third at Redcar 16 days ago (first run at the trip) she was still beaten nearly 10l in a three-runner race; headgear dispensed with today; not solid.
runners.comment: No improvement for handicaps as yet; headgear off today; dropping down the weights

runners.name: Wedonttellthetruth
runners.spotlight: Improved for the step up to 1m4f, addition of headgear (cheekpieces) and switch to handicaps when a well-backed winner at Wolverhampton in March; similar form when sixth at Kempton three weeks later but latest run (blinkered first time) was disappointing; upped to 1m6f for his turf debut and he needs more for it.
runners.comment: 1m4f AW win in March (well backed); below par latest; upped in trip for turf debut

runners.name: Femme Fatale
runners.spotlight: The second of her three runs as a 2yo was respectable; 18-1 and well beaten on her handicap debut here (1m2f, good) nine weeks ago; could leave that behind now stepping up 4f in trip and sporting a hood for the first time.
runners.comment: Down the field on handicap debut (1m2f, good) nine weeks ago; bred to appreciate this test

runners.name: Song Of The Stars
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt when making a successful turf debut at Lingfield 12 days ago (extended 1m3f, good to firm); stepping up in trip could unlock further progress and she holds leading claims up 5lb.
runners.comment: Made a winning turf debut 12 days ago (11.6f); longer trip could unlock further progress

races.race_name: Events At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Toptime
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last August (good; has won on soft); has often run well or won following a break and he did so again when involved in a close finish at Thirsk last week on first run since October; weighted to find another race and likely to be involved.
runners.comment: C&D win in August; goes well fresh and close 3rd on return; weighted to find another race

runners.name: Thornaby Pearl
runners.spotlight: Has course form; 6f and 5f wins have come on a variety of tracks and he's won two of his six 5f runs since having wind surgery, most recently at Pontefract (good; acts well on soft) on Monday; unpenalised for that apprentice win under Oliver Carmichael; leading claims.
runners.comment: Won 2 of 6 starts over 5f since having wind surgery; unpenalised for Monday's win

runners.name: Charging Bull
runners.spotlight: Maiden after ten starts, seemingly best on the AW but he could never get involved on apparently favourable terms in a Wolverhampton classified event recently and has work to do back on turf.
runners.comment: 0-10; seemingly best on AW but never involved in Wolverhampton classified last time out

runners.name: Albegone
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins, including over C&D; best turf form in 2026 came when involved in a tight finish on this track in April (good; has won on soft) and although some so-so efforts since, a recent Musselburgh second suggested he has a good run in him back here.
runners.comment: C&D winner; close up here in April and he can be involved back on this track
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

runners.name: Wrestling Revenue
runners.spotlight: Flexible regarding the going; had mixed results since winning at Pontefract (5f, soft) last October, but he wasn't far away after making the running at Ripon recently and is now 2lb lower than that last winning mark; probably needs a bit more.
runners.comment: Mixed results since 5f win at Pontefract last October but now 2lb lower

runners.name: High Opinion
runners.spotlight: Two C&D wins on soft and good ground; below best in the autumn and has needed both runs in 2026 but he's run two of his best races for Paul Mulrennan (1-3 on him) who is back up today; on a handy mark and a revival could be imminent.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins; below best in the autumn; has needed both runs this year; on a handy mark

races.race_name: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Classified Stakes

runners.name: South Shore Island
runners.spotlight: Exposed and frustrating maiden for Amanda Perrett but regularly ran well in stronger races than this; promising stable debut at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) last week, keeping on for a close third; one of the likelier types.
runners.comment: 25-race maiden but last week's stable debut was encouraging; one to take seriously

runners.name: Rambuso Creek
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate for one of his ability; drops into first 0-50 classified event and has good 7lb claimer booked; should give a good account.
runners.comment: Drops into his first 0-50 classified event with a good 7lb claimer booked; should go well

runners.name: Timebar
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; just touched off in the corresponding event 12 months ago (good ground); absent until returning over 5f here last month, a race where he weakened in the final furlong having raced on his own up the far rail; goes without the blinkers today but still more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Second in this race last year; sharper for last month's return; one to be interested in

runners.name: Green Valentine
runners.spotlight: Won 6f handicaps at Hamilton (soft; 25-1) and Newcastle (AW; 66-1) for Alan Brown in the latter part of 2025; short of that level in two runs for new stable; cheekpieces return having been left off over 7f last week.
runners.comment: Two big-price wins for Alan Brown last year; yet to shine for new yard

runners.name: Baileys Ontherocks
runners.spotlight: 0-10 and not progressing, with three runs for Tony Carroll yet to arrest her slide; cheekpieces offer some encouragement for better but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden for whom the addition of cheekpieces needs to have a big impact

runners.name: Twilight Serenade
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed after eight attempts; drops back to 6f for his first attempt in classified company but he is not a solid option.
runners.comment: Peak RPR of 45 from his eight starts; drops in trip/grade but he is not a solid option

runners.name: Charcon
runners.spotlight: Not a great deal to celebrate in her six runs to date, the latest of them at Carlisle on Monday (6f, soft); new cheekpieces need to spark a surge of improvement.
runners.comment: Low-level form in six starts, the latest on Monday; now tried in headgear

runners.name: Spirited Dancer
runners.spotlight: Disappointing since her early-season 2yo days; plummeted down the weights as a consequence and latest Bath second (5.7f, good), her first run at this level, was more encouraging; this looks a deeper race though and she'll need to back it up.
runners.comment: Drop to 0-50 company saw her finish second at Bath last week; needs to back it up

runners.name: She's A Goldigger
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden; too free when fifth of nine at Carlisle (7f, good) a fortnight ago and now drops in trip with a hood fitted; each-way shout if returning to her best.
runners.comment: 0-14 but has shown some promise; drops in trip with a hood fitted; each-way shout

runners.name: Yesnia
runners.spotlight: Just modest form in her five starts, including in a classified event at Wolverhampton ten days ago; the return to 6f isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Modest form in five starts, including at this level latest; needs more back up to 6f

runners.name: Piddie's Pearl
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in her six starts, the latest over 7f at Doncaster last week; improvement essential.
runners.comment: Big prices and well beaten in all six starts; improvement essential

runners.name: Posh Maisie
runners.spotlight: Regressive 17-race maiden; last of eight on last week's return from a year on the sidelines; best watched.
runners.comment: Regressive 17-race maiden who was well held on last week's return from a year off

races.race_name: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Pisiffik Ginger
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory filly made a satisfactory debut when fourth of eight at Fairyhouse (6f, good) a fortnight ago, doing best work at the finish; bred to get much further so this trip should help.
runners.comment: Satisfactory debut when fourth of eight at Fairyhouse (6f, good); will get further

runners.name: Cashel Queen
runners.spotlight: Price increased to 46,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to French winner Gregolimo (1m2f/10.5f inc 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 108); dam French 6f-7.5f winner; stuck to her task well when seeing off Lily Bear in 7f Naas barrier trial last month; top connections and receives 4lb from that filly here.
runners.comment: Beat Lily Bear in 7f Naas barrier trial last month; top connections and receives 4lb

runners.name: Epilogue
runners.spotlight: Awtaad filly cost 6,500gns; half-sister to seven winners; dam 1m-8.6f winner (inc AW; 102), half-sister to Dubai World Cup winner African Story; respectable third in 7f barrier trial at Naas last month; yard 2-42 with 2yos.
runners.comment: Cheap buy; respectable third in 7f barrier trial at Naas last month; yard 2-42 with 2yos

runners.name: Spanish View
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; 3,000euros yearling; half-sister to winners Nice Spirit (French 1m; RPR 83) and Artic Power (US 6f turf); dam placed 1m in France (78); stable 0-165 with 2yos; best watched.
runners.comment: Fifth foal; 3,000euros yearling; half-sister to several winners; stable 0-165 with 2yos

runners.name: Caoch The Piper
runners.spotlight: Awtaad filly cost 40,000euros as a yearling; dam unraced half-sister to Sea Silk Road (1m6f Group 1), out of German 7.5f 2yo Listed winning half-sister to 1m6f Group 3 winner Oriental Eagle; market can guide.
runners.comment: Awtaad filly cost 40,000euros; dam unraced half-sister to Sea Silk Road (Group 1 winner)

runners.name: Celtic Moon
runners.spotlight: Thunder Moon filly; 20,000euros vendor yearling; dam French 10.5f/1m3f winner, half-sister to 1m5f Flat and useful 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner River Frost, out of unraced half-sister to 1m Group 2 winner Passing Glance; yard 0-14 with 2yos this year.
runners.comment: Thunder Moon filly cost 20,000euros; yard 0-14 with 2yos this year but still respected

runners.name: Lily Bear
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; 25,000euros yearling; half-sister to six winners inc Solent Gateway (1m2f-2m Flat inc AW, RPR 103; 2m hurdle); dam 7.5f-1m2f winner (inc Italian Group 1); travelled notably well when runner-up to Cashel Queen in 7f Naas barrier trial last month; hooded; respected.
runners.comment: Travelled notably well when runner-up in 7f Naas barrier trial last month; hood; respected

runners.name: Dear Madam
runners.spotlight: Sottsass filly cost 21,000euros as a foal; half-sister to five winners inc Galles (UAE 1m1f-9.7f dirt), Longden (US 8.5f dirt/1m1f turf) and Golden Valour (7f-10.6f inc AW;); dam 1m winner; stable's sole 2yo runner this season won so worth monitoring.
runners.comment: Cost 21,000euros as a foal; half-sister to five winners; stable 3-13 with 2yos

runners.name: Cloughleagh Bridge
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal cost 37,000euros; sister to Key To Cotai (Listed-placed 5f 2yo/7f; RPR 97); dam 5f-7f 2yo winner (85); stable 0-63 with 2yos.
runners.comment: 37,000euros foal; sister to Key To Cotai (Listed-placed); stable 0-63 with 2yos

runners.name: Star Glory
runners.spotlight: Offered something to build on with never-nearer 5.25l sixth of 12 on debut at the Curragh (6f, good) after getting upset in the stalls; should improve for that and could go well if acting on softer ground.
runners.comment: Sixth of 12 on debut at the Curragh (6f, good); could go well if acting on softer ground

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Expert Dancer
runners.spotlight: Consistent in maidens, making the frame on all four occasions (best RPR on soft to heavy); ran below best on handicap debut at the Curragh (1m, good) in a visor (disgarded); each-way player back on a softer surface.
runners.comment: Consistent in maidens including on soft; below best on handicap debut on good; e-w player

runners.name: Cromac Quay
runners.spotlight: Bit green but got the message late on when third on her course debut (1m, good to yielding; runner-up won the Oaks last weekend); beaten odds-on favourite when second at Naas next time (1m, good); sets a good standard.
runners.comment: Just behind subsequent Oaks winner on debut; second at Naas lto; sets a good standard

runners.name: Slaney View
runners.spotlight: Showed ability on all three maiden starts at 5f-7f, including on soft; failed to beat a rival home in C&D Group 3 next time; nearer last than first on Curragh handicap debut; cheekpieces need to spark a revival.
runners.comment: Showed ability in maidens but has seemingly gone backwards since; cheekpieces
runners.owner: Molly & Paul Willis Racing, Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Make The Tide
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace to finish midfield on her course debut (1m, good); made little impression 11 days later at Dundalk (7f); looks one for another day.
runners.comment: Midfield on her course debut (1m, good); made little impression 11 days later at Dundalk

runners.name: Blooming Rose
runners.spotlight: Blue Point filly cost 850,000euros as a yearling; closely related to smart 1m-1m2f winner Botanical (RPR 117), half-sister to last weekend's impressive Coronation Cup winner Bay City Roller; dam 1m2f-1m6f winner (inc Listed); easy winner of 7f Naas barrier trial last month; could be exciting.
runners.comment: Blue Point filly cost 850,000euros; half-sister to Bay City Roller; easy barrier trial win

runners.name: Siesta Senorita
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal cost 8,000euros; dam well beaten 6f-1m, half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 3 winner Royal Razalma, out of Listed-placed 7f winner; can only be watched.
runners.comment: Fourth foal cost 8,000euros; dam well beaten 6f-1m; can only be watched

runners.name: Lecale Breeze
runners.spotlight: Second foal; 1,000euros yearling; closely related to Italian 7.5f winner Tosca Galaxy but makes little appeal here; tongue-tied.
runners.comment: 1,000euros buy; closely related to Italian 7.5f winner Tosca Galaxy; makes little appeal

runners.name: Ebony Reign
runners.spotlight: Ten Sovereigns filly; 32,000euros yearling; sister to 6f 2yo winner Leonardo Dax (RPR 91), closely related to 5.7f turf/6f AW winner Apache Star (81); fourth to Blooming Rose in Naas barrier trial; others preferred.
runners.comment: Ten Sovereigns filly; 32,000euros yearling; fourth to Blooming Rose in Naas barrier trial

runners.name: Miss Moore Cen
runners.spotlight: Midfield finish on debut at the Curragh (6f, soft); not a bad effort when fifth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good) 13 days ago but likely to fall short in this lineup.
runners.comment: Fifth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good) 13 days ago but likely to fall short here

runners.name: Sydney Dollars
runners.spotlight: Mehmas filly cost 40,000euros as a yearling and was heavily supported late on in the betting on 1m debut at Naas last month but lost all chance with a slow start; still early days and she's worth monitoring.
runners.comment: Well-backed for Naas debut but slow start costly; still early days so watch betting

runners.name: Banish Misfortune
runners.spotlight: First foal; 1,000euros vendor yearling; dam unraced sister to 1m-1m4f Flat and 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Snookered, out of 1m winner; others preferred.
runners.comment: Very cheap purchase; dam unraced; others preferred

runners.name: Into Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: Respectable fourth of 12 on her Cork debut last month (7f, soft to heavy) when beaten 8.5l; will need to take a significant step forward to land this.
runners.comment: Respectable fourth of 12 on her Cork debut but will need to take a big step forward here

runners.name: Monamie
runners.spotlight: Never recovered from a slow start on Roscommon debut; 400-1 when tailed off at Fairyhouse; will likely be aimed at handicaps.
runners.comment: 400-1 when tailed off at Fairyhouse; will likely be aimed at handicaps

runners.name: Mullacurry Maid
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to stable's nine-time winner Ducky Mallon but no impact in three maidens last year.
runners.comment: Half-sister to stable's 9-time winner Ducky Mallon but no impact in 3 maidens last year

runners.name: Quafftide
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run on debut at the Curragh in May last year but regressed at Naas in October; 243 days off.
runners.comment: Not a bad run on debut at the Curragh in May last year but regressed at Naas in October

races.race_name: GAIN The Advantage Series Handicap

runners.name: Dinamine
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner, including over this trip on heavy at Gowran Park last year off 8lb lower; latest win at Bellewstown (1m, g/y) in August; satisfactory return over inadequate trip at Navan 26 days ago; 3lb drop generous and player with ground likely to suit.
runners.comment: Two-time winner, inc on heavy; won at Bellewstown in August; ok return over 6f lto; player

runners.name: Only One Scobie
runners.spotlight: Benefitted from return to soft ground, being dropped in the handicap and far more positive tactics when making all over a mile here seven days ago on his eighth start, winning cosily by 5l; has to recover quickly and probably will try dominate from the front again; obvious contender under a penalty but more on his plate down in trip.
runners.comment: Made all here seven days ago easily; more on his plate down in trip but leading player

runners.name: Green Kite
runners.spotlight: Best run in six efforts came in Killarney maiden (1m, soft) in October when 5.5l to Lady Lilac; mild signs of promise in two runs this term, latest at Gowran Park 22 days ago when beaten 3.25l into fourth over 7f on good to yielding in a claimer; dropped 4lb and Colin Keane takes over for yard in good form; could be on a nice mark and each-way chance.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; ran fine in claimer lto; best run came on soft last year; each-way shout

runners.name: Meriden
runners.spotlight: Improved for switch to handicaps last month and dropped back to this trip to good effect when scoring at Down Royal; followed up under a penalty dropped to 6f at the Curragh eight days ago easily by 3.5l in a good time; now 14lb higher but she's lightly raced and hard to know her ceiling; not guaranteed to handle soft but leading player nonetheless.
runners.comment: Won Curragh h'c easily eight days ago; 14lb higher but lightly raced; big chance

runners.name: Minaun View
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 16 starts; best efforts have come on the AW; has found some consistency on turf the last three times; beaten 2l into sixth at Down Royal (7f, gd) 13 days ago despite being keen; dropped 1lb; soft ground a question mark.
runners.comment: Maiden after 16 runs; has found consistency; only beaten 2l lto; soft ground a question

runners.name: Fortuity
runners.spotlight: Improved for switch to handicaps and good ground when second at Roscommon last month before going one better at Fairyhouse (7f, gd) 14 days ago when making all to beat Elly Bay by 0.5l, showing good attitude after racing keenly; up 5lb and improving but soft ground a negative.
runners.comment: Improving sort who toughed it out off 5lb lower lto; progressing, soft ground a negative

runners.name: Lar Na Coille
runners.spotlight: Best run in three maidens came at Navan (6f, soft) in October when beaten 7l into sixth by Michael's Well; down the field at the Curragh 53 days ago; bred to appreciate step up in trip and opening mark looks reasonable.
runners.comment: Best run in three maidens at Navan in Oct on soft; bred to enjoy 7f, h'cap debut
runners.owner: Daragh McCormack & Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Loingseoir
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent veteran, 7-74 in his career and made the frame on three visits to this track, including seven days ago when 5l fourth to Bella Isabella; handles soft; needs things to fall right given how he's ridden; drawn wide; each-way chance if granted a solid pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Consistent veteran; ran well here lto when fourth; needs a good pace; handles soft; chance
runners.owner: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick

runners.name: Are You In Or Out
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last April for Paul Stafford off 13lb lower; positive start for new yard when staying on strongly to land Fairyhouse handicap (6f, good) 14 days ago by 0.5l off a seven-month layoff; up in trip and 4lb higher; handles soft; should go well again.
runners.comment: C&D winner; won on debut for new stable 14 days ago; up in trip; 4lb higher; handles soft

runners.name: Sayfa Fad
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well to win Bellewstown handicap (1m, gd) in August but never landed a blow in two starts after; trainer has more obvious chance with Only One Scobie; off 254 days.
runners.comment: Never landed a blow in two starts after Bellewstown win last year; off 254 days

runners.name: Racing Royalty
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner on soft ground in 2023; signs his turn could be near again when placed at Galway in September before two below-par runs over a mile; drop in trip and ground will suit; off 241 days.
runners.comment: Two-time winner on soft; placed at Galway in Sept but two poor runs after; off 241 days

runners.name: Zheleznaya Dama
runners.spotlight: Much-improved effort on fifth run when going close to landing a gamble at Down Royal (7f, gd) in September, beaten 2l into third by Sovereign Thought; travelled through the race like she was well treated; up 4lb and off 279 days; soft ground an unknown.
