runners.comment: Much-improved when 2l third in h'cap in Sept, travelled powerfully; up 4lb, off 279 days

runners.name: Shoot To Kill
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year on good ground, including at Roscommon off 80; bounced back to form with encouraging third at same track last month before backing that up under positive ride at Down Royal 13 days ago when 1.5l second to Faoladh; definitely capable off this mark but soft ground a concern.
runners.comment: Back to form the last twice and won off 10lb higher last year but soft ground a concern

runners.name: Tawaazon
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner, latest over a year ago at Naas (6f, gd) off 3lb higher before good third at the Curragh; returned from layoff to be beaten 10l in claimer at Fairyhouse 14 days ago; that leaves him with plenty to find up in trip.
runners.comment: Nine-time winner, latest May 2025; below-par on return in claimer lto; plenty to find

runners.name: Charming Whisper
runners.spotlight: Won five times in Britain, latest in August; shaped well a couple of times on the AW for this stable but didn't beat a rival home the last twice on turf, slowly away both times; down 3lb.
runners.comment: Five-time winner in Britain; was running okay on AW but last two turf runs poor; down 3lb

runners.name: Free Solo
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, including here as a 2yo; ran well here twice last summer but has been out of form this term; 11th of 15 at Roscommon 24 days ago; down 4lb and hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Course winner who ran well here twice last year; out of form this term; hard to recommend

races.race_name: Ballycorus Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Native Warrior
runners.spotlight: Rediscovered his best with a visor added last summer, landing three handicaps, including at Ascot (7f, soft) off a mark of 103; cracking return when beaten 0.5l by Dreamliner in Group 3 at Saint-Cloud in March on soft; didn't come on as expected when beaten 7.5l into seventh by Wannabe Royal in Listed race at Naas 25 days ago; nothing got into it from off the pace that day so shouldn't be judged too harshly; handles soft and the one to beat.
runners.comment: Won three competitive h'caps in 2025; second in G3 on return; beaten fav lto; one to beat

runners.name: East Hampton
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in Madrid Handicap at Naas (7f, soft) last year; best subsequent run came when beaten a neck by Diego Velazquez in Group 2 at the Curragh over same trip; started this year positively when 1.5l second to Big Gossey in Listed Gladness 75 days ago, deserving extra credit for where he came from; at his best on soft and big run expected.
runners.comment: Excellent comeback when shaping well in second in Listed race 75 days ago; enjoys cut

runners.name: Oh Cecelia
runners.spotlight: Cork maiden winner over 5f on good for Jack Davison last year; positive start for new stable when winning Naas handicap (5f, soft) in March, added another win in conditions race at Cork (5f, soft); cracking run when a head second to the very smart Havana Anna in Lacken Stakes 25 days ago having raced alone stands side; chance on that but step up in trip not guaranteed to suit.
runners.comment: Clear career-best lto when second to Havana Anna; handles soft but 7f not sure to suit

runners.name: Wannabe Royal
runners.spotlight: Limerick maiden winner on soft; followed a poor run over 6f at Cork with a career-best at Naas (7f, gd) 25 days ago when making all in Listed race; needs to build on that and not guaranteed to do so; likely to go forward.
runners.comment: Career-best when making all in Listed Naas race 25 days ago; not sure to build on that

runners.name: Redemption Road
runners.spotlight: Tipperary maiden winner last year over extended 7f on yielding; backed it up when second to the smart Limestone on heavy at Listowel; returned this term with a clear career-best when 3.25l third to Thesecretadversary in Group 3 over C&D; didn't back that up last time out at the Curragh but each-way chance with conditions to suit down in trip.
runners.comment: Returned this year with good third in G3; below that lto but ew chance on best form

runners.name: Celestial Orbit
runners.spotlight: Ran nine times for Ollie Sangster, winning twice as a 2yo; best run for that stable came at Saint-Cloud (1m, heavy) when second in Listed; career-best on debut for this stable when winning Group 3 at the Curragh in April, worth more than the 0.5l winning margin suggests; backed up that run in less testing conditions when beaten 3l into seventh in Group 2 18 days ago; big shout back on favoured ground; down in trip.
runners.comment: Career-best on stable debut when winning G3; good run on gd ground lto; big chance on soft
runners.owner: J P Magnier/Mrs P Shanahan/Mrs M V Magnier/K MacLennan

runners.name: Audience
runners.spotlight: Group 1 Lockinge winner in 2024 and Group 2 winner at Goodwood the same year; on a losing run since and ran poorly in Dubai in three starts this year; didn't fare any better at Haydock 33 days ago, fading tamely; hard to be positive about him currently.
runners.comment: G1 winner in 2024; seems a fading force based on last four efforts; hard to be positive

runners.name: Zodiac Bear
runners.spotlight: Looked potentially smart when storming 4.25l clear in C&D maiden last year on second start; improved on that to be beaten 2l into third in this race last year, shaping particularly well on unfavoured side of the track; off since; open to plenty of improvement and a big player.
runners.comment: Bolted up in C&D maiden last year; excellent run when third in this after; off since

races.race_name: Bulmers Live Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Moyassr
runners.spotlight: Boasts a fine AW record (four wins) and gained a first turf victory under this rider at Fairyhouse (1m2f, good) a fortnight ago; must be respected off the back of that but sole run on soft previously was a poor effort and this is a much tougher assignment; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: AW specialist gained first turf win at Fairyhouse lto; much tougher race; prefers good

runners.name: Coeur d'Or
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner but not since Irish Cambridgeshire win of 2024; definite encouragement on penultimate run at this track (1m2f) when beaten 3.5l in sixth; kept on well to take 1l third at Cork (10.5f, good to yielding) on latest; off a winnable mark and drop in trip is in this veteran's favour.
runners.comment: No win since 2024 Cambridgeshire but definite signs of life last two starts; chance
runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & S P O'Connor

runners.name: Abbey Actress
runners.spotlight: Solid form in handicaps for John O'Donoghue; good efforts in handicaps at the Curragh (1m, soft to heavy) and at this course (1m2f, yielding to soft; had Couer d'Or behind) on first two runs for this stable; outclassed in Listed grade at Gowran but a big player if the ground deteriorates.
runners.comment: Outclassed in Listed grade at Gowran but a big player if the ground deteriorates

runners.name: Vega's Muse
runners.spotlight: Added second turf win when keeping on well to comfortably land course handicap (1m, good to yielding) on seasonal return; follow-up attempt under 7lb penalty came too quickly on softer ground; tailed off last time at Naas.
runners.comment: Impressed here on seasonal return on good to yielding; has struggled off revised marks

runners.name: Slieve Binnian
runners.spotlight: Multiple turf/AW winner including over C&D; ran respectably returned to Bahrain in January but soundly beaten there twice since; prefers a quicker surface than what's forecast which is a concern.
runners.comment: Multiple turf/AW winner including over C&D; prefers a quicker surface than what's forecast

runners.name: Clear Quartz
runners.spotlight: Record of 6-36 on turf with three of those at his beloved Galway; rounded off last year with midfield finish at that venue from a tough draw; handles the mud but usually needs the comeback run.
runners.comment: Record of 6-36 on turf with three of those at his beloved Galway; might need the run here

runners.name: Musical Act
runners.spotlight: Won two Kempton novices at 7f/1m in 2023; close up in Grade 3 at Keeneland in April 2024; finished no better than fourth in seven runs (six on AW) since joining this yard and soft ground looks a negative.
runners.comment: No better than fourth in 7 runs (6 on AW) since joining this yard and soft a negative

runners.name: Morse
runners.spotlight: Sole win came at this course (1m, heavy) off 6lb higher in April 2024; regressive last season; returns from a layoff with plenty to prove; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Sole win came at this course (1m, heavy) in 2024 but has plenty to prove now
runners.owner: Mrs John M Weld & N J Hitchins

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Marmeladova
runners.spotlight: Went close in 1m Navan maiden on the third of five starts last season; a revelation on reappearance at Ballinrobe, winning very easily from an 85-rated favourite; poor run last time at Gowran but could be well treated on handicap debut for all soft ground is an unknown.
runners.comment: Excellent win at Ballinrobe; could be well treated on handicap debut for all soft a worry

runners.name: Electric Beauty
runners.spotlight: Course winner; has been busy this year; fit from AW when winning at the Curragh (1m, heavy) in March; has largely remained in good form since, given a forceful ride at Roscommon (10.5f) on latest when finishing second; can go well dropped 1lb.
runners.comment: Course winner; won on heavy at the Curragh in March; largely in good nick since; chance

runners.name: She's Ideal
runners.spotlight: Maiden but she's generally been running consistently this season; early traffic problems when third at Fairyhouse last month puts her right in the picture; not at her best there six days ago; blinkers; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Early traffic problems when third at Fairyhouse last month puts her right in the picture

runners.name: Anvika
runners.spotlight: No win since June 2024; standout effort last year a fine second in premier fillies' handicap at this venue (1m, good to yielding); 5lb lower here but hard to fancy on this season's efforts and testing conditions also a worry.
runners.comment: No win since June 2024; second in premier fillies' handicap here last year; poor of late

runners.name: Lamberella
runners.spotlight: Failed to threaten in three maidens at 7f-1m but did catch the eye making late gains at Limerick on third start (7f, yielding); made no impact on 7f handicap debut at the Curragh last month; up in trip.
runners.comment: Modest ability in maidens; no impact on handicap debut at Curragh last month; up in trip

runners.name: Rocket Robyn
runners.spotlight: Best run came when close second in Gowran maiden (9.5f, heavy) last autumn; well below that level since, including on handicap debut at that venue on latest; tongue-tie is reached for now.
runners.comment: Best run last year in a Gowran maiden on heavy; two poor runs this year; tongue-tie

runners.name: Tachos
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) around this time last year from 8lb lower; missed the break on her return at Gowran 10 days ago when 16l behind Spanish Temptress; wouldn't want it too soft.
runners.comment: Missed the break on her return at Gowran 10 days ago when 16l behind Spanish Temptress

runners.name: Justenzia
runners.spotlight: Sole win off 15lb lower at Clonmel last September (9.5f, soft); runner-up first two starts this term at 9.5f-1m5f; tailed off at Gowran 10 days ago; forecast rain a plus; could bounce back.
runners.comment: 15lb higher than last win; runner-up twice this term; amiss last time; rain a plus

runners.name: Speckled Meadow
runners.spotlight: Course winner; added her fourth handicap win at Naas in October (10.5f, yielding) off 63; out of her depth on return at Cork before midfield in Naas handicap last month; chance if near her best.
runners.comment: Out of her depth on return before midfield in Naas handicap; chance if near her best

runners.name: Spanish Temptress
runners.spotlight: Went up an aggregate 21lb for three handicap wins last season; unlucky in two of her last three outings, beaten a nose at Limerick (1m, yielding) in April and just failed at Gowran (9.5f, yielding) 10 days ago; enjoys testing conditions; another big run on the cards.
runners.comment: Three handicap wins last season; unlucky twice this term; handles the mud; every chance

runners.name: Finsceal Annie
runners.spotlight: Won twice last season, both at this venue (7f, good to soft) including on this card; remains 5lb above her last win and her first two runs of the season offered little, but the yard had two winners at Roscommon on Monday so watch the betting.
runners.comment: Won twice here last year over 7f; still 5lb above last winning mark but stable in form now

runners.name: Great Mover
runners.spotlight: Only win from 21 starts came on heavy ground at Gowran (9.5f) last September from 5lb lower; failed to retrieve her best form in first four outings this term, but beaten only 1.25l when third at this course (7f, soft) last week and the step up in trip is a plus.
runners.comment: Gowran winner on heavy last September; back to near her best at this track last time

runners.name: Lady Chartwell
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f Chelmsford handicap for Charlie Johnston last year; inconsistent record subsequently and she beat just one rival home on her first two runs for current yard; Irish turf debut.
runners.comment: AW winner for Charlie Johnston; no show in two AW runs for this yard

runners.name: Codice
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on the Flat in France last year without winning but has been poor in three Irish runs, including over hurdles twice for this yard; best watched.
runners.comment: Showed ability on Flat in France without winning but has been poor in three Irish runs

races.race_name: Sandyford Business District Handicap

runners.name: Desert Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Maiden in 17 starts but was placed on the AW three times this year and ran well from the front 83 days ago from a wide draw; low mark but hard to have too much confidence back on turf up in trip, especially on soft ground.
runners.comment: Shaped consistently well on AW this year; turf form uninspiring however, esp on soft

runners.name: Porsche Lad
runners.spotlight: Been running consistently well on the AW for over a year, landing two handicaps, latest in November off this mark; beaten favourite 104 days ago when 2.75l behind Daonethatgotaway over 1m4f; four turf runs have been poor and soft ground unlikely to suit; drawn wide.
runners.comment: Very consistent on AW, gaining two wins; four turf runs been poor, soft unlikely to suit

runners.name: Launch Time
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on eighth run when landing a mile handicap at this course in April (good), beating Cleopatra's Needle by 0.75l; ran to a similar level at Cork 48 days ago when beaten 3l into fourth; 7lb rise seemed to anchor him that day but another good run can be expected dropped 1lb.
runners.comment: Course winner over a mile here in April; ran to a similar level off 7lb higher lto; chance

runners.name: Oreeda
runners.spotlight: Hard to find encouragement in four handicap runs thus far; not an obvious contender.
runners.comment: Hard to find encouragement in four handicap runs thus far; not an obvious contender

runners.name: Rodeo Blues
runners.spotlight: Three runs of little consequence in maidens but made more of an impression on handicap debut when beaten 6.25l into fourth at Gowran Park (9.5f, yielding) ten days ago, still looking green; likely to improve with racing and could figure for top stable.
runners.comment: No impact in maidens; fared better on h'cap debut when fourth lto; likely improver
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited

runners.name: Staywitherjohnny
runners.spotlight: Step in the positive direction on seventh start when beaten 4.25l into third by Majestic King at Gowran Park (1m, good); failed to back that up at same track 22 days ago when ninth; steps up in trip, lightly raced and trainer in good form.
runners.comment: Failed to back up positive run lto at Gowran; lightly raced, up in trip; trainer in form

runners.name: Nibras Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Followed a poor effort at Cork with a 33-1 surprise win at Killarney (1m, good) 30 days ago, holding on by a short head with Secret Magician in third; stays well and finished second three times in testing conditions last year so a definite player off 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Got head back in front marginally lto; enjoys soft so a definite player off 4lb higher

runners.name: Hale Bopp
runners.spotlight: 2-34 on turf, last turf win in May 2022; been running fine this year without really threatening to win; placed at Gowran Park (1m, good) two starts ago; well held ten days ago; no obvious reason why he'd warrant support off same mark.
runners.comment: Been running fine on turf without threatening to win; poor run lto; hard to fancy

runners.name: Roderick
runners.spotlight: Five-time winning veteran; last win came at Naas (1m, yielding) last July off 1lb higher; on a decent mark but not much encouragement to be taken from return to turf 63 days ago when 12th at Gowran Park.
runners.comment: Last win came in July off 1lb higher; not enough encouragement 63 days ago back on turf

runners.name: Rockbury Lad
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner who shaped well in a C&D handicap in August when eighth off 7lb higher mark; placed on the AW twice in last three runs but below-par effort here over a mile 27 days ago; soft not likely to suit.
runners.comment: Shaped well in C&D h'cap last year off 7lb higher; below form here lto; ground concerns
runners.owner: P E Ryan & J G Shanahan

runners.name: Crackles
runners.spotlight: Little promise in five maiden starts and down the field on handicap debut at Listowel 11 days ago; up in trip.
runners.comment: Little promise in five maiden starts and down the field on handicap debut at Listowel

runners.name: Nephin Mountain
runners.spotlight: Two best runs in six starts came at Gowran Park over 7f on soft, finishing fourth in pair of handicaps in April either side of a less appealing run at Bellewstown; handles soft and shaped as if this trip could be within reach last time.
runners.comment: Two best runs came on soft over 7f at Gowran in April; shapes as if trip should be okay

runners.name: Ella's Gold
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and improved for switch to handicaps when 1.5l second at Naas (1m, good) in September; went close again when beaten a neck up 4lb at Gowran Park after on heavy; ran poorly on return six days ago at Fairyhouse but slight drop back in trip and testing conditions could see her play a part.
runners.comment: Went close on heavy in Oct; poor return six days ago but drop in trip and ground will suit

runners.name: Slaney Swagger
runners.spotlight: Maiden on turf after 20 runs; placed five times last year on good and heavy but finished on a low note with a below-par run on the AW; off 183 days and makes stable debut dropped in trip; will handle the ground and is capable off this mark.
runners.comment: Maiden, five placed efforts last year; off since poor run on AW in Dec; handles all ground

runners.name: Khormor Syd
runners.spotlight: Maiden in ten runs but has threatened to break it the last two times when runner-up at Gowran Park (9.5f, soft) and Limerick (1m4f, yielding); unlucky last time having hit the front and nailed on the line; up another 3lb but ground will suit and can go close again.
runners.comment: Threatening to break maiden last twice; beaten 0.75l lto, unlucky; up 3lb; big run likely

runners.name: Medieval Night
runners.spotlight: Encouraging run on handicap debut after a break when 1.5l second over C&D in August off 8lb higher; nothing of note in five starts after but fared better under positive tactics when 7l eighth at Gowran Park (1m, yielding) 10 days ago; still a fair bit to do.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D last August but struggled since; better lto but fair bit to find still

runners.name: Garryowen Green
runners.spotlight: Towards the back and little impression in three maiden runs; makes handicap debut off basement mark and hard to know what to expect.
runners.comment: Towards the back and little impression in three maidens; makes handicap debut off low mark

runners.name: Colugo
runners.spotlight: 0-16 on the Flat, six on the turf; consistent on the AW during the winter and failed to make an impact in three hurdle runs since; ground a question mark; off ten days since finishing eighth in Listowel maiden hurdle.
runners.comment: Consistent on AW during winter, placed three times; ground a question mark

runners.name: Folk Warrior
runners.spotlight: Showed little in first six starts on the Flat and over hurdles; plugged on reasonably well when fifth over 1m4f at Dundalk 16 days ago but hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Fared better on the AW lto having showed little in six starts prior; hard to recommend

runners.name: Rampage
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner, last turf win in 2023 over this trip at Galway on soft; two AW wins since; beaten 9.5l into seventh at Gowran Park 22 days ago (1m, good); cheekpieces back on and handles soft but mark looks a bit high.
runners.comment: Last turf win in 2023 on soft; beaten 9.5l in Gowran h'cap 22 days ago; mark looks high
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney

runners.name: One For Teigan
runners.spotlight: No sign of promise in six starts in maidens from 7f to 1m4f; market support might pique interest but one to avoid otherwise on handicap debut.
runners.comment: No promise in six starts in maidens from 7f to 1m4f; market support might pique interest

runners.name: Secret Magician
runners.spotlight: Last turf win came at Naas in August 2024 over 7f on good; signs he could capitalise on sliding mark when close third at Killarney (1m, good) in May but disappointing favourite at Listowel 11 days ago; step up in trip should suit but becoming hard to predict.
runners.comment: Followed a good run with poor one at Listowel lto; extra trip to help but hard to predict

races.race_name: CopyBet And Worcester Here All Year Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Beorma
runners.spotlight: Fast-improving mare who has been on the rampage over the last fortnight, winning two 2m chases and two hurdles (2m4f/2m) on good ground; carries 7lb penalty for Tuesday's success over fences at Southwell but did that pretty easily; obvious contender, providing she's not derailed by significant rain.
runners.comment: On the rampage over the last fortnight, winning four times; obvious contender

runners.name: Miss Pearl
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 16 runs but hasn't been with current stable long and wasn't beaten far when fourth of six on chasing debut at Ffos Las (3m, good to soft) three weeks ago; may benefit from this drop back in trip and has the services of Sean Bowen; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on recent chasing debut and has the services of Sean Bowen here

runners.name: Best Mate Dave
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when last of four in Warwick hunter chase (3m, good) last month but managed only fifth of seven in a subsequent point; back in a handicap chase for stable debut; quite tricky to weigh up at the moment; seems best suited by good ground.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when last of four in a hunter chase last month; makes stable debut here

runners.name: Les's Destiny Star
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; 0-6 over hurdles and needs to bounce back from a poor run but his third of 14 at Ffos Las (3m, good to soft) in April was a good effort; drops back in trip and sports first-time cheekpieces on chasing debut; could have a say if back on song.
runners.comment: Pulled up last time but penultimate hurdle run gives him a shout on this chasing debut

runners.name: Rockvale
runners.spotlight: 0-10 under all codes but was clear second to a next-time-out winner over fences at Uttoxeter (2m5f, good) last month and has each-way claims at least off same mark here; placed in Irish points on soft ground.
runners.comment: Clear second to a next-time-out winner at Uttoxeter last month; has same mark here

runners.name: Wings On Wheels
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed under rules and was seemingly aided by new cheekpieces when second of four at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in March; that form isn't anything to get excited about, though, and he was well held when third at Perth (2m4f, good to soft) last month; now tries a visor; forecast rain probably not ideal.
runners.comment: Second in cheekpieces two starts ago; safely held since; now tries a visor

runners.name: Emberscombe
runners.spotlight: Won two four-runner points this spring but has poor record under rules (0-14) and makes only limited appeal here.
runners.comment: Won two four-runner points this spring but has poor record under rules (0-14)

runners.name: Feet On The Ground
runners.spotlight: No threat to easy winner Beorma when third of five on chasing debut here (2m, good) two weeks ago but that run was still quite encouraging, and she might be better suited by this longer trip; won't mind if the ground softens; one to consider.
runners.comment: No threat to Beorma when third here on chase debut but this longer trip might suit better

runners.name: Dynamite Defense
runners.spotlight: Placed in two low-grade AW handicaps for Olly Murphy in February/March but was last of six when back over hurdles here (2m, good) for new stable last month and is not bred for chasing.
runners.comment: Last of six over hurdles here on recent stable debut and not bred for chasing

runners.name: Kings Order
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six hurdle runs, most recently when beaten about 28l in Chepstow handicap (2m, good to soft) in March, but his Irish point second last spring offers a ray of hope on this chasing debut.
runners.comment: Unplaced all six hurdle starts but last May's Irish point second offers a ray of hope

races.race_name: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Grand Clermont
runners.spotlight: Won seven chases (most on heavy ground) when based in France and came good for David Pipe at the second attempt, with wide-margin success at Uttoxeter (3m, good) last month; up 10lb in higher-grade race here but still commands respect.
runners.comment: Prolific chaser in France and a wide-margin winner on second British start; respected

runners.name: Ideal Des Bordes
runners.spotlight: Had very mixed record in 2025 but was creditable second at Newbury (3m2f, good; second run in blinkers) on latest outing in December and has strong claims if in similar form here; well suited by good to soft ground and was a point winner on yielding to soft.
runners.comment: Absent since Newbury second in December but a major player if in similar form again here
runners.owner: Mrs C Hanbury & Mrs H Mackenzie Smith

runners.name: Presentandcounting
runners.spotlight: Good-ground specialist with nine chase wins on his CV; returned from break with two respectable runs this spring and has edged his way back down to a good mark but the forecast rain is the obvious worry.
runners.comment: Good-ground specialist; both runs this spring were respectable; forecast rain a big worry

runners.name: Excello
runners.spotlight: Now 4lb lower than when creditable third of 17 over the big Aintree fences (2m5f, good to soft) in November but his subsequent form has been underwhelming; drops in grade here but still needs to up his game and has presumably been overlooked by Nico de Boinville; seems versatile ground-wise.
runners.comment: On good mark and drops in grade but needs to up his game; overlooked by Nico de Boinville

runners.name: Bagheera Ginge
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who returned from seven-month break with 7l win in first-time visor at Stratford (2m6f, good to firm) last month; only 4lb higher here but the market principals in that race disappointed and the form is of questionable value; raced mainly on good ground but has won on good to soft and been placed on soft.
runners.comment: Made all for 7l win at Stratford last month but the market principals there disappointed

runners.name: Disco Davis
runners.spotlight: Very inconsistent for Dan Skelton last season but ran well from the front when third here (2m4f, good) on stable debut three weeks ago and won't mind if the ground is much slower today (has won on heavy); unexposed over this sort of trip; a possible if in the right mood again.
runners.comment: Inconsistent for Dan Skelton last season but ran well for new stable three weeks ago

runners.name: Samuel Spade
runners.spotlight: Lacked fluency and was ultimately well beaten over C&D (good) on chasing debut a fortnight ago, after a layoff, but significant positives can still be drawn from that performance and he ought to cope better than some of these if the ground softens; can be dangerous if in a better rhythm today.
runners.comment: Ultimately well beaten over C&D on chase debut but positives can still be taken from it

runners.name: My Bobby Dazzler
runners.spotlight: Ended last season in good form, winning at Hereford (3m1f, soft) before good second at Haydock (3m1f, good), and ran quite a bit better than eventual 20l defeat suggests when fourth in Cartmel marathon (3m5f, good) last month; no surprise if he features here.
runners.comment: Ended last season in good form and certainly didn't run badly at Cartmel last month

runners.name: Stumps Or Slips
runners.spotlight: In excellent form in hunter chases in April, winning twice in good style on good ground (3m/2m7f); can't be ruled out after those exploits but faces tougher assignment on this handicap return and probably won't be favoured by the forecast rain.
runners.comment: Won two good-ground points in April; in very good nick but faces tougher task here

runners.name: Peaky Boy
runners.spotlight: Disappointing for new stable last season but appreciated the ease in grade when respectable third of six in quite valuable contest at Uttoxeter (3m, good) last month and won't mind what the weather does here; contender if able to build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Appreciated ease in grade when fair third at Uttoxeter in May; versatile ground-wise

races.race_name: CopyBet Royal Ascot Bog From Sunday Mares' NH Flat Race (Conditional/Amateurs) Cat 1 Elim) (GBB)

runners.name: Unflappable
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to fairly useful chaser Pure Carbon; made promising debut when clear third of ten under positive ride at Newton Abbot (good) last month and ought to put that experience to good use here.
runners.comment: Clear third of ten under positive ride on last month's debut at Newton Abbot; respected

runners.name: Queens Glance
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam low-grade 2m Flat winner, half-sister to stable's fair hurdler I'm A Starman; wears a tongue-tie; other newcomers look more interesting on paper.
runners.comment: First foal from a low-grade 2m Flat winner; other newcomers appeal more on paper

runners.name: Maximore
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in two points this year but represents astute Irish stable and is worth a glance in the betting before this rules debut; hood fitted.
runners.comment: Pulled up in two points this year but represents astute Irish stable; check the betting

runners.name: Lucy Glitters
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister to a point winner; dam bumper/2m5f hurdle winner, half-sister to bumper and 2m6f/3m hurdle winner Smart Mover; one of two newcomers from this stable; wears a hood.
runners.comment: Out of a bumper winner; one of two newcomers from the stable; wears a hood

runners.name: Tottie's Moon
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unplaced in bumper/over hurdles, sister to useful jumper Mac Tottie; wears a tongue-tie; stable does well in bumpers.
runners.comment: First foal from sister to useful jumper Mac Tottie; stable does well in bumpers

runners.name: Dora's Double
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam point winner, out of unraced half-sister to Midlands Grand National winner Russian Trigger; stable has healthy strike-rate in bumpers (12%); market may guide.
runners.comment: First foal from a point winner; stable has healthy strike-rate in bumpers; market helpful

runners.name: Evenly
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam maiden pointer/well beaten in bumper, half-sister to useful jumper Bobs Law; stable does pretty well in bumpers (12% strike-rate); a possible.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to useful jumper Bobs Law; stable does pretty well in bumpers

runners.name: Honour Me
runners.spotlight: £1,000 3yo; ninth foal; half-sister to winners Persian Time (useful hurdle/chase), Restandbethankful (bumper/jumps), Thermusa (hurdle) and Little Jack (bumper); dam maiden; with a good stable; plausible contender.
runners.comment: With a good stable and is a half-sister to useful jumper Persian Time; plausible contender

runners.name: Lady Of Tarsus
runners.spotlight: Third foal from unraced sister to high-class bumper/hurdle winner Kilcooley; beaten before unseating rider at penultimate fence in heavy-ground Irish point in February; may fare better in this different contest.
runners.comment: Beaten when unseating rider on Irish point debut; might fare better under rules

runners.name: Crack Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: 23,000gns yearling; second foal; half-sister to a fair sprinter; dam well-connected 6f winner; not an obvious type for bumpers but represents good Flat stable and has presumably inherited stamina from his sire.
runners.comment: Out of a 6f winner; not an obvious type for bumpers but represents a good Flat stable

runners.name: Eagles Promise
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam fair 2m hurdle winner, sister to smart bumper/jumps winner Super Duty and useful jumper Grandads Cottage; one of two newcomers in this field for Tom George (4-22 in bumpers in last five seasons).
runners.comment: Out of a sister to smart bumper winner Super Duty; stable has bumper winners

runners.name: Matilda Maid
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to two point winners; dam unplaced in bumpers, half-sister to stable's Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Coneygree and high-class jumper Carruthers; might be a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to stable's Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Coneygree

runners.name: Pearl Cove
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; half-sister to low-grade chase winner Eightytwo Team; dam useful hurdler/chaser, half-sister to Flat winner/useful hurdler Dangerously Good; rare bumper runner for the stable.
runners.comment: Half-sister to low-grade chase winner Eightytwo Team; rare bumper runner for the stable

runners.name: Poppieholla
runners.spotlight: Third foal; sister to 2m3f hurdle winner Briefly, half-sister to 2m hurdle winner Sammy's Guarantee; dam bumper winner/placed 2m6f hurdle, half-sister to useful bumper/jumps winner Thomas Brown; makes some appeal on pedigree but stable not prolific in bumpers.
runners.comment: Makes some appeal on pedigree but stable not prolific in bumpers

runners.name: Liberty Leader
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to smart jumper Libberty Hunter but showed only minor promise when fifth of nine at Bangor (good) last month and needs to take a big step forward here.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise when fifth of nine at Bangor (good) last month

races.race_name: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Maiden Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)

runners.name: Shamsat
runners.spotlight: Won on the Flat in France last year (heavy ground) and has been placed on both British hurdling starts, more recently when second of ten in similar race to this over C&D three weeks ago; likely contender again.
runners.comment: Second of ten in similar race to this over C&D last month; likely contender again

runners.name: Premier Tenor
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut on the Flat in France last October (good to soft); unable to justify favouritism when third (just behind Shamsat) over C&D (good; first-time hood/tongue-tie) three weeks ago but that still goes down as a promising stable/hurdle debut, and he has obvious scope for progress.
runners.comment: Flat winner in France; promising third over C&D on recent stable/hurdle debut

runners.name: Babs
runners.spotlight: Doesn't help herself by tending to race too freely but won good-ground Stratford bumper in the autumn and ran big race in defeat over hurdles at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) a fortnight ago; should cope if the ground eases; can make her presence felt.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who ran big race in defeat over hurdles last month; can feature

runners.name: Drombeg Lad
runners.spotlight: Went close in good-ground course bumper a year ago and was placed on both hurdling starts last summer, the latter over C&D; has fitness to prove after 284-day absence and ground slower than good would be new to him but he still enters each-way calculations.
runners.comment: Absent since C&D third last August but still enters calculations

runners.name: Nightflyer
runners.spotlight: Won soft-ground Catterick bumper for Adrian Keatley on debut in January; ran well in only one of three subsequent bumpers for current stable but faced tough task in Aintree festival Grade 2 on latest occasion; should have a future over hurdles and won't mind the forecast rain.
runners.comment: Had mixed record in bumpers but won one on soft ground; should have a future over jumps

runners.name: Trouville
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat racer who was placed in Class 3 AW handicap (2m; RPR 89) on latest outing last summer; changed hands for 13,000gns in the autumn; has form on good and soft ground; will be dangerous at this level if he takes adequately to hurdling at the first attempt.
runners.comment: Useful Flat racer; dangerous at this level if he takes to hurdling for new stable

runners.name: Lucky Diamond
runners.spotlight: Blotted copybook when veering right and unseating rider soon after the start of a recent bumper but the form of his third here last October (good ground) has worked out quite well; could have a say if all goes to plan on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Blotted copybook last time but form of his bumper third here in October worked out okay

runners.name: Luckonthemoor
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m4f AW winner (RPR 76), half-sister to useful jumper Smarty Wild; probably best watched on belated debut.
runners.comment: Out of a half-sister to useful jumper Smarty Wild; probably best watched on belated debut

runners.name: Amalfina
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on the Flat for Karl Burke but showed some fair form at up to 1m5f and seems versatile ground-wise; sold for 7,000gns in the autumn; makes stable/hurdle debut after eight-month break.
runners.comment: Fair at up to 1m5f on the Flat (albeit without winning); makes stable/hurdle debut

runners.name: Hollysaway
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at big odds on both hurdling starts (2m/2m1f); needs subsequent wind op to have made a massive difference.
runners.comment: Well beaten at big odds on both hurdling starts (2m/2m1f); had wind op last month

races.race_name: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Maiden Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)

runners.name: Kernie d'Airy
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning slow-ground French bumper on debut in November 2024 but was second on hurdle debut at Hereford (2m3f, good) in March and might not have been suited by the idiosyncratic track when below that form at Fakenham (2m4f, good) last month; drops back in trip with tongue-tie added here; has a fighting chance.
runners.comment: Second on hurdle debut and may not have handled Fakenham last time; can bounce back

runners.name: Purple Owl
runners.spotlight: Fair form when placed twice in novice company towards end of 2025 (2m, good/soft) but well beaten in two 2m4f handicaps this year; drops back in trip here; needs to get career back on track.
runners.comment: Fair form when placed in two novice hurdles but well beaten in two handicaps since

runners.name: Fiskardo
runners.spotlight: Led on post to win good-ground Perth bumper in April, after a long layoff, and wasn't discredited when well-held second over hurdles at Ffos Las (2m, good to soft; winner had useful bumper form) last month; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Narrow bumper winner in April and not discredited when well-held second on hurdle debut

runners.name: Just Call Me Buzz
runners.spotlight: Well held when third on hurdling debut in April but displayed improvement and lost out only by a nose when second over C&D (good) three weeks ago; might encounter slower ground today but still a major player.
runners.comment: Displayed improvement and went very close over C&D last month; major player

runners.name: Mystic Wind
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam placed 1m3f AQPS Flat/well held only hurdles start, closely related to a French 2m3f chase winner, half-sister to to two French AQPS Flat winners; represents a top stable but it's unnerving to see a hood fitted on debut.
runners.comment: Represents a top stable but it's unnerving to see a hood fitted on debut

runners.name: Dancingondiamonds
runners.spotlight: Failed to build upon last spring's quite promising Irish Flat debut when unplaced odds-on favourite in AW maiden in January but was given a wind op soon afterwards and has joined good stable for hurdling career.
runners.comment: Showed some promise on the Flat in Ireland; with good stable for hurdling career
runners.owner: The Soldiers Of Fortune Iv & Partner

runners.name: Angel Of My Dreams
runners.spotlight: Easily won a point bumper in March 2025 (good to soft) but did not show much in three rules bumpers (remote when second last month); significant improvement required on hurdle debut; tongue-tie removed.
runners.comment: Won a point bumper last year but did not show much in three rules bumpers; hurdle debut

runners.name: Minella Blizzard
runners.spotlight: Out of a well-connected mare but was pulled up on Irish point debut in April and also struggled in a recent novice hurdle; wears first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Pulled up on Irish point debut in April and also struggled in a recent novice hurdle

runners.name: Bacardi Blue
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in Newton Abbot bumper last September and returns to racing under rules after well-held second in two-finisher point last month; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Well held when second in two-finisher point last month; look elsewhere
runners.owner: D Summersby J May W Dodd

races.race_name: CopyBet For Your Daily Profit Boost Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Sunray Shadow
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo is thriving of late and he readily followed up his hurdling success at Huntingdon (3m1f, good to firm) in 2m7f handicap chase here (good) two weeks ago; up 8lb but still looks the one to beat back hurdling.
runners.comment: Readily followed Huntingdon hurdles win with chase success here since; the one to beat
runners.owner: S T Racing - The Founders' Syndicate

runners.name: Shallow River
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward on just his second run for Olly Murphy when landing 3m1f handicap hurdle at Carlisle in October 2024; went without his usual cheekpieces after 17 months off when a never-dangerous sixth in 2m4f Hexham handicap last month; can strip sharper now with headgear refitted.
runners.comment: Off 17 months before sixth at Hexham last month; can strip sharper with headgear refitted

runners.name: Ballintubber Boy
runners.spotlight: Looked rusty after seven months off when last of seven in 3m1f handicap hurdle at Huntingdon 23 days ago; this fairly useful winning hurdler can take a step forward now.
runners.comment: Last at Huntingdon 23 days ago; this fairly useful winning hurdler can take a step forward

runners.name: American Mike
runners.spotlight: Very smart 3m-winning chaser for Gordon Elliott in 2024/25; also scored over fences for current yard at Perth (3m) in April but beat just one when tried in cheekpieces in 2m4f handicap chase at Uttoxeter 18 days ago; reverts to hurdles off a 7lb lower mark but still has something to prove.
runners.comment: Won at Perth in April but beat just one at Uttoxeter since; back hurdling with it to prove

runners.name: Gwennie May Boy
runners.spotlight: A smart 3m-winning hurdler for Dan Skelton in 2024/25; only raced twice for Olly Murphy last season and not seen out since being pulled up in Long Walk Hurdle at Ascot in December; has it to prove starting out for another new yard here.
runners.comment: Off since pulled up in Long Walk Hurdle at Ascot in December; has it to prove for new yard

races.race_name: Step Into Luxury With Purple Granite Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Raffles Nobu
runners.spotlight: Won in France in 2023; struggled in three starts for Nicky Henderson and limited since joining this trainer; attracted market support but weakened from the second-last when third of five at Huntingdon (2m4f) 17 days ago; dropping back in trip is a sensible move.
runners.comment: Limited since joining this trainer (0-9) and beaten 15l over 2m4f at  Huntingdon latest.

runners.name: Emerald Time
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a Warwick bumper and glimpses of ability over hurdles, none more so than when falling two out at Ludlow (2m, soft) in February; lacked the pace to challenge on handicap debut at Aintree (2m1f, good) but still unexposed; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Lacked pace to challenge on handicap debut but still fairly unexposed; tongue-tie added.

runners.name: Play Pretend
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (three ran) last July and beaten a neck over 2m4f here (similar mark) last October; below par in two starts this year, lacking fluency when beaten 36l at Bangor (2m4f, good) latest; a possible on peak form.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck over 2m4f here last October; below par in two starts this year.

runners.name: Son Of Hypnos
runners.spotlight: Won at Galway (2m, good) in July 2024; downhill thereafter, ending time in Ireland competing on the Flat; third of five at Exeter (2m; 33-1) on second start for this trainer but tough to fancy on strength of his other two efforts.
runners.comment: Third of five at Exeter (2m) on second start for yard but not so good since; hard to fancy

runners.name: Bobbarelli
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner; still seeking first rules win (0-6); likes to race prominently, weakening on the run-in when last seen over hurdles at Taunton (2m3f, good) in April; went down fighting over fences at Stratford 12 days ago; player.
runners.comment: Still seeking first rules win (0-6) but went down fighting over fences at Stratford latest

runners.name: Strong As Steel
runners.spotlight: 0-11; pulled up in three of his last five runs; returns from six-month break on just his third outing for trainer (also represented by Alex The Great); an easing mark aside, there aren't too many positives.
runners.comment: Maiden (0-11); pulled up in three of his last five; back from a six-month break.

runners.name: Alex The Great
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner (1m6f-2m); well beaten in three spins over hurdles; lowly rated for handicap debut but he needs a spark; stablemate of Strong As Steel.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three spins over hurdles; lowly rated for handicap debut.

runners.name: Western Soldier
runners.spotlight: Won at Wincanton (1m7f, good; from 2lb lower) last November, but lost his way and unseated in March; stayed on into modest fourth at Hexham (2m; beaten 12l) three weeks ago, though; slightly more needed.
runners.comment: Stayed on into modest fourth at Hexham (2m; beaten 12l) three weeks ago; more needed.

runners.name: Barafundle Bay
runners.spotlight: Won on the Flat (about 1m3f) in June 2024; well beaten all three starts over hurdles, including when jumping left on occasions at Ffos Las (2m4f; handicap debut) three weeks ago; now 3lb lower; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Well beaten all three starts over hurdles, including at Ffos Las (2m4f) on handicap debut

runners.name: Belcamo
runners.spotlight: Broke his duck over jumps at Fakenham (2m, good to soft) in January; that wasn't a particularly strong contest and he has been absent since; now 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Broke his duck over jumps at Fakenham (2m, good to soft) in January; now 3lb higher.

runners.name: Privilege
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on the Flat at Tipperary (7.5f) in June 2021; twice runner-up at Newton Abbot (2m3f, good) this time last year; weakened quickly on latest start at Bath last August; isn't impossible, but reasons to oppose.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up at Newton Abbot (2m3f) 12 months ago; off since well beaten last October

runners.name: Katana
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over C&D on final start for Nicky Henderson last May, and again on first start for this trainer (6-4 favourite) the following month; badly out of sorts since and remains winless; handicap mark has plummeted accordingly; clear risks attached.
runners.comment: Runner-up on first start for trainer over C&D (6-4 fav) last June; fortunes dipped since.

races.race_name: CopyBet For Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: October Hill
runners.spotlight: Got back on the up with a breakthrough success in 2m4f handicap at Ffos Las 22 days ago; she must enter calculations back up 2lb.
runners.comment: Back on the up with a breakthrough win at Ffos Las 22 days ago; must enter calculations

runners.name: Kom Tu Voudras
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced ex-Irish hurdler who made a winning start for Faye Bramley in 2m4f Huntingdon maiden in February; built on that with an excellent second of seven on handicap debut at Warwick (2m5f) last month; winner has gone in again so she's a player off an unchanged mark; cheekpieces  added.
runners.comment: Won at Huntingdon for new yard before excellent Warwick second; player off the same mark

runners.name: Hello Cello
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over C&D last summer before gaining a breakthrough success in 2m3f handicap at Fontwell in October; lightly raced since but she posted a good third of 12 in 2m5f Warwick handicap last month so needs considering off the same mark.
runners.comment: Good third in 2m5f Warwick handicap last month so needs considering off the same mark
runners.owner: Harry Fry Racing Syndicate Hello Cello

runners.name: Belle Le Grand
runners.spotlight: Easily resumed winning ways at Newton Abbot (2m1f) in May and not discredited off a 7lb higher mark when sixth of ten in 2m1f handicap at Cartmel 19 days ago; not out of things back up in trip.
runners.comment: Won at Newton Abbot; not disgraced at Cartmel latest; not out of things back up in trip

runners.name: All Inn Hand
runners.spotlight: Scored at up to 3m over hurdles at Stratford and fences at Hexham early last summer; well below par both runs this spring after 11 months off, though, pulled up in 3m2f hurdle at Stratford in May; has it to prove now.
runners.comment: Off 11 months and well below par both runs this spring, pulled up at Stratford 25 days ago

runners.name: Shesonnet
runners.spotlight: Gained a first victory on her handicap debut at Warwick (2m5f) in May but she beat only two in similar company at Stratford (2m6f) 12 days ago; she needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won at Warwick in May but beat only two at Stratford 12 days ago; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Janworth
runners.spotlight: Returned from an absence in fine form last summer when a two-time scorer, including over C&D; back from nine months off here with yard going very well so she merits serious consideration given her good record when fresh.
runners.comment: C&D scorer last summer; merits serious consideration given her excellent record when fresh

runners.name: Kittys Glance
runners.spotlight: Underwent wind surgery before landing 2m4f maiden at Huntingdon (first-time hood) in March; not discredited under a 7lb penalty when second of five in 2m4f Hereford novice 30 days ago; she may do better still now going into handicaps with few miles on the clock.
runners.comment: Won at Huntingdon; not disgraced with Hereford second after; may do better on h'cap debut

runners.name: Politacus
runners.spotlight: Winless since April 2024 and she came in a remote second of seven back hurdling in 2m Uttoxeter handicap 18 days ago; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Winless since 2024; remote second at Uttoxeter 18 days ago so needs to take step forward

runners.name: Portetta
runners.spotlight: Returned from four months off with a below-par tenth in 2m1f handicap at Down Royal last month; plenty more is required if she's to gain a maiden victory here.
runners.comment: Below-par tenth at Down Royal last month; plenty more needed if she's to gain a maiden win

races.race_name: British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Wilbur
runners.spotlight: 160,000euros yearling by Kingman out of a 1m juvenile winner; promising 2.75l fourth of ten on last month's Newbury debut (6f, good; 16-1, not getting the best of runs); 7f should suit; improvement likely.
runners.comment: 160,000euros yearling; promisng fourth of ten on Newbury debut; should improve; contender

runners.name: Rogue Passion
runners.spotlight: 90,000euros yearling; first foal; by Sioux Nation out of a 7.5f/10.3f winner; worth monitoring in the market on her debut.
runners.comment: 90,000euros yearling; first foal of a 7.5f/10.3f winner; worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Collateral Damage
runners.spotlight: By Too Darn Hot; 270,000gns foal; first foal; dam 1m AW winner; interesting newcomer who should be checked in the market.
runners.comment: 270,000gns foal; by Too Darn Hot out of 1m AW winner; interesting newcomer; check market

runners.name: Sou'wester
runners.spotlight: Has shown a degree of promise, notably when 6l fifth of seven on his Redcar debut (7f, good; 40-1); pulled hard and hung left at Beverley last time (7.5f, good to firm; 16-1) but this track should suit him better.
runners.comment: Yet to reach the frame, but has shown some promise in his two runs; this track should suit

runners.name: Leave The Bag In
runners.spotlight: Ran green when eighth on her debut in a York novice (6f, good; 16-1) but didn't improve on that when seventh in a valuable Thirsk novice last time (7f, good; 8-1); this is easier.
runners.comment: Fair runs in two decent novice events and has possibilities now dropped in grade

races.race_name: Bowel Cancer Screening Programme Saves Lives Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lady Gormire
runners.spotlight: Beaten 12l when runner-up on debut for Kevin Ryan; joined this trainer for 12,000gns, and made a solid start when runner-up in maiden fillies' race over C&D (6-1) three weeks ago; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Solid start for yard when runner-up over C&D three weeks ago; enters calculations.

runners.name: Korbut
runners.spotlight: Tried to pinch it at Southwell (7f fillies' novice; 33-1) on second start, but couldn't hold off the winner; ran to a similar level when fifth over that trip at Wolverhampton since; now tackles 6f for turf debut.
runners.comment: Some respectable efforts, including when runner-up at Southwell (7f; 33-1) in April.

runners.name: Nursia
runners.spotlight: Cosy Newcastle AW winner (6f novice; 13-8) in January; that form isn't easy to evaluate, the runner-up winning two Class 5 handicaps since, but the odds-on favourite (third) has yet to reappear; carries a penalty for that, and returns to turf as a gelding after a break.
runners.comment: Carries a penalty for January's cosy Newcastle AW novice win (6f; 13-8); gelded since

runners.name: Alpha Platoon
runners.spotlight: 55,000gns yearling; second foal of 6f AW winner (RPR 100); gelded half-brother to the 5f juvenile scorer Ain't Nobody (including Listed; 114); best watched on debut, with various question marks lingering.
runners.comment: 55,000gns yearling; out of 6f AW winner; probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Two Commanders
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to four winners; did as well as could be expected when a weakening third over 7f here (maiden, good to firm; 17-2) 12 days ago; dropping back to 6f should be fine, although the ground conditions are now likely to be slower; chance.
runners.comment: Fair third over 7f here (maiden, 17-2) 12 days ago; dropping back to 6f should be fine.

runners.name: Sugar Bon Bon
runners.spotlight: Dam was a 6f-7f winner; fifth of nine on Southwell debut (6f novice; 200-1, beaten 9l) six weeks ago; could build on that now switched to turf, but others tick more boxes at this stage of his career.
runners.comment: Fifth of nine on April Southwell debut (6f novice; 200-1, btn 9l); others preferred.

runners.name: Havana Oaks
runners.spotlight: Fifth of nine on Newcastle AW debut in a 6f fillies' maiden (7-1) last October, but took a backward step at Southwell (5f; 33-1, last of five) a few weeks later; off for 224 days since so she couldn't be the pick on this turf debut; monitor only.
runners.comment: Fifth on Newcastle AW debut (6f) but took a backward step at Southwell last October

runners.name: Lamlash Bay
runners.spotlight: Struggled on Redcar debut (6f, good; 150-1) last November, but better at Southwell (6f; 80-1, fourth of eight, beaten 7l) the following month; eligible for a handicap mark after this comeback run.
runners.comment: Bit better at Southwell (6f; 80-1, btn 7l) in December; soon eligible for a handicap mark.

races.race_name: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap

runners.name: Powernap
runners.spotlight: 0-10; runner-up in added tongue-tie over C&D (Havin A Flyer fourth) 12 days ago; occupied that same position at Wetherby (about 5f, good to firm) subsequently; consistent but has had plenty of chances.
runners.comment: Runner-up in her last two starts, including over C&D, but has had plenty of chances (0-10)

runners.name: Gwen Tennyson
runners.spotlight: Didn't offer much on first two starts but showed speed and she improved when third, to one who now has a mark of 72, in C&D novice (50-1) 12 days ago; switches to a handicap at an appropriate level; possibilities.
runners.comment: Improved form when 3rd in C&D novice (50-1) latest; now switches to a handicap

runners.name: Havin A Flyer
runners.spotlight: 0-17; didn't look entirely straightforward when fourth over C&D (Powernap runner-up) 12 days ago; she could strip fitter for that comeback run though, and it's possible the added cheekpieces may suit.
runners.comment: 0-17; didn't look straightforward when fourth over C&D recently; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Sparkling Wine
runners.spotlight: Sent off at big prices all three starts; only seventh of eight (beaten over 12l) in a 6f fillies' novice at Ripon (33-1) under a month ago; improvement necessary, even at this lowly level, on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Big prices and modest form in three starts; improvement needed now handicapping

runners.name: Brain Freeze
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a tight finish at Redcar (5f; good to firm; 12-1) latest; that form has taken a couple of hits (second and third soundly beaten since) and has more to do from this 3lb higher mark; the prospect of rain is also a legitimate concern.
runners.comment: Won in tight finish at Redcar (5f; good to firm; 12-1) latest; more to do from 3lb higher.

runners.name: Tanaka
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in two starts for Kevin Ryan, and has done little better since joining David O'Meara; it's tough to envisage her troubling Powernap or Havin A Flyer on recent C&D evidence; focus elsewhere.
runners.comment: 0-5; tough to envisage her troubling Powernap or Havin A Flyer on recent C&D evidence.

runners.name: Saxon Grace
runners.spotlight: Heavily supported on stable debut (sixth) on Wolverhampton AW in March and was only headed deep inside the final furlong when fourth at Carlisle (6f, good; 11-2) a fortnight ago; this sharp 5f could prove ideal.
runners.comment: Headed late when fourth at Carlisle (6f, good; 11-2) a fortnight ago; sharp 5f can suit

runners.name: Enjoy The Night
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three novice/maiden events and it was a similar tale in added tongue-tie on handicap debut at Thirsk (5f; 28-1) last September; has left the Quinns since; hard to be interested in him back from an absence unless the market speaks favourably; has been gelded.
runners.comment: No joy on handicap debut at Thirsk (5f; 28-1) last September; has left the Quinns since

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: Isle Of Lismore
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in May 2021 (heavy); wasn't in top form last season so has dropped to a good mark but he was well beaten on soft at Haydock on reappearance.
runners.comment: C&D winner (heavy); not in top form in 2025 and well held on reappearance; on a good mark

runners.name: Lucius Aurelius
runners.spotlight: Record here is 2231, his win coming over 5f in April (good); can be forgiven a below-par effort next time after being restless in stalls; interesting back here.
runners.comment: Has an excellent track record (2231), his win coming over 5f in April; should go well
runners.owner: John Kelly, John McNeill & Kevin Mowatt

runners.name: Equity Law
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 1lb higher last September; two good runs when third at Ripon in April (5f, good) and at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) last month but he hasn't done as well since.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2025; two decent runs earlier in the season but not so good the last twice

runners.name: Winchurch
runners.spotlight: Effective at 5f/6f on turf and AW; easy winner at Nottingham in April (6f, good) for which he went back up 8lb; not so good on AW next time but has a chance back on turf.
runners.comment: Effective at 5f/6f; easy Nottingham winner in April; below par latest; chance back on turf
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Le Beau Garcon
runners.spotlight: Four wins, the latest on reappearance at Ayr last May (5f, good); fair fourth on last month's Ripon reappearance (5f, good), although that form hasn't worked out; good second to Equity Law here last September and, with his stable going well, he's worth considering.
runners.comment: Ayr winner last May; fair fourth on reappearance; stable going well; worth considering

runners.name: Glory Fighter
runners.spotlight: Two of 11 wins have been over C&D; latest success was at Nottingham (5f, good) in April but rather disappointing since; has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Two of his 11 wins have been over C&D; Nottingham winner in April but below par since

runners.name: Arctic Summer
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; won over 6f at Chantilly in 2024 (very soft); rather disappointing since joining present yard, including at Carlisle on Tuesday when tried in cheekpieces (retained).
runners.comment: Won at Chantilly in 2024; below par for this yard and cheekpieces no help on Tuesday

races.race_name: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Lightning Tiger
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; won over 1m4f here on handicap debut (soft) last October; fair form since and could go well, although he didn't run to his best on only previous try at this trip; hood on for second time.
runners.comment: Winner over 1m4f here on h'cap debut; fair efforts since;  stamina to prove over this trip

runners.name: Sixpack
runners.spotlight: Three good runs this season, notably when winning off 3lb lower over 1m4f at Doncaster early last month (good to firm); stays 1m6f well; not disgraced on good to soft last time and one with a chance.
runners.comment: Three good runs this term, including 1m4f Nottingham win; stays 1m6f well; chance

runners.name: Baileys Khelstar
runners.spotlight: Missed 2025 but proved he has retained his ability with two good runs this year, notably when second to an in-form stablemate of Sixpack over 2m at Newbury last time (good to firm); acts on any ground; can go well.
runners.comment: In good form after long break this term, including when 2nd at Newbury last time
runners.owner: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)

runners.name: Military Air
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; winner at Lyon Parilly on his debut in 2024 (1m, very soft) and third in a Longchamp Listed race (1m4f, good to soft) last year; bit disappointing since 45,000gns purchase last autumn but capable of better than he has shown in Britain.
runners.comment: Ex-French; third in a Listed race in 2025; 45,000gns buy; yet to hit top form for new yard

runners.name: Robert Johnson
runners.spotlight: Has won seven of his 23 races at up to 2m2f, including two over 1m4f here in 2023 and 2024; creditable 2l third of seven at Newbury on latest start in April 2025 when with Tracy Waggott; now 4lb lower and an interesting contender on stable debut despite 426-day absence.
runners.comment: Two of seven wins came here; good 3rd in April 2025; off since; could go well for new yard

runners.name: Gibside
runners.spotlight: Two course wins, including this race off 8lb lower in 2024 (good to firm); highly tried and not been at his best this season but quite interesting down in grade.
runners.comment: Dual course winner, including this race in 2024; not at his best this term; down in grade

races.race_name: Racing Again 22nd June Handicap

runners.name: Vince Le Prince
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner on soft ground, the latest off a 6lb higher mark in October 2024; plenty of sound runs since (placed over C&D on four occasions); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner on soft ground; has run some good races here this season; each-way chance

runners.name: Habrdi
runners.spotlight: Only turf win was in a Brighton novice (6f, soft) for Jane Chapple-Hyam in October 2023; however, he has run two sound races to be placed at Ayr on last two starts (7f, good to firm); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Only turf win was in 2023 but has an each-way chance after two sound runs at Ayr in May
runners.owner: Mrs S D Pearson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Uppercase
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden; tends to pull hard but sound run when third at Yarmouth last time (7f, good); can make the running which would be an asset round here; interesting.
runners.comment: Lightly raced maiden; good 3rd at Yarmouth last time; can lead so interesting here

runners.name: Qazaq
runners.spotlight: Won his first three races, over 7f/1m on the AW, when with Roger Varian; hasn't won since, although was beaten only a head when second at Musselburgh in April; not done quite as well since and was behind Tattie Bogle at Ayr last time (7f, good to firm).
runners.comment: Three AW wins for Roger Varian; not won since and was behind Tattie Bogle last time

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: Winner off a 2lb lower mark at Musselburgh in April (7f, good); two sound placed efforts on last two starts; solid run on only previous run here; chance.
runners.comment: Musselburgh win in April; two sound runs since; close 3rd only previous run here; chance

runners.name: Jenni
runners.spotlight: Musselburgh winner off 8lb higher in May last year (7f, good); below par both runs in 2026; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Musselburgh winner in May 2025; below par both runs this year; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Pouting
runners.spotlight: Ex-Richard Hughes; bought for 14,000gns last July; 11-race maiden; tailed off on only start for this yard (second run after wind op) last August; off since; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Ex-Richard Hughes; 14,000gns buy; 0-11; tailed off on stable debut last August; off since

runners.name: In A Hurry
runners.spotlight: Won a Thirsk maiden last June (7f, good); good third over this C&D next time (good to firm) but has been very disappointing on her last four starts (two of them this season) so has plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: Maiden winner last June; sound run here next time but disappointing on last four outings

runners.name: Coconut Bay
runners.spotlight: Three wins last June/July including this race on good ground and another handicap over C&D on good to firm in July; creditable third at Ayr early last month but not so good since; makes a quick reappearance after a below-par run on soft ground on Monday (6f).
runners.comment: Two C&D wins in 2025, including this race; good 3rd three runs back but not so good since

runners.name: Imperial Guard
runners.spotlight: Only win was at Kempton in 2024 when with Andrew Balding; ran three sound races over 5f/6f for Michael Dods in May 2025; mainly disappointing since, including with cheekpieces (left off today) last time.
runners.comment: AW winner for Andrew Badling; three fair runs early last season; disappointing since
